docs.title: 1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build complete practical and production-level expertise in CrewAI for agentic systems.

docs.title: 1.2 CrewAI Positioning
docs.text: CrewAI focuses on collaborative agents, crews, and event-driven flows for production automations.

docs.title: 1.3 Core Building Blocks
docs.text: Agents, Tasks, Crews, Processes, Flows, Memory, Knowledge, Tools, and Event Listeners.

docs.title: 1.4 Runtime Philosophy
docs.text: Composable primitives enable both fast prototyping and hardened production execution.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support automation combines specialist agents and flow routing for triage and escalation.

docs.title: 02 - Installation, Environment, and CLI Project Setup
2.1 Installation Path
docs.text: Use uv-based installation and project scaffolding recommended in CrewAI docs.

docs.title: 2.2 Environment Strategy
docs.text: Manage API keys and model/provider settings via environment variables.

docs.title: 2.3 CLI Workflow
docs.text: Use CLI scaffolds to generate standard project structure and configuration templates.

docs.title: 2.4 Project Layout
docs.text: Typical layout includes agents.yaml, tasks.yaml, crew definition, flows, and tools.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team spins up a new automation project with consistent config and deployment-ready layout.

docs.title: 03 - Agents: Design, Attributes, and Direct Kickoff
3.1 Agent Concept
docs.text: An agent is an autonomous unit with role, goal, tools, memory, and execution constraints.

docs.title: 3.2 Agent Attributes
docs.text: Important controls include max_iter, allow_delegation, reasoning, tool access, and callbacks.

docs.title: 3.3 Creation Patterns
docs.text: Define agents via YAML (recommended) or code-level declarations.

docs.title: 3.4 Direct Interaction
docs.text: kickoff() supports direct agent usage without full crew orchestration.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Research agent with delegated search tools returns scoped findings for a product brief.

docs.title: 04 - Tasks, Outputs, Guardrails, and Human Input
4.1 Task Role
docs.text: A task defines what must be done, by which agent, with expected output contract.

docs.title: 4.2 Output Modes
docs.text: Task outputs can be raw text, JSON, or structured Pydantic outputs.

docs.title: 4.3 Guardrails
docs.text: Single or chained guardrails validate and transform outputs before acceptance.

docs.title: 4.4 Human Review
docs.text: human_input adds approval checkpoints for sensitive or high-impact decisions.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Compliance task blocks release until guardrail checks and human review both pass.

docs.title: 5.1 Crew Definition
docs.text: A crew coordinates agents and tasks under an explicit process strategy.

docs.title: 5.2 Lifecycle Hooks
docs.text: Use kickoff hooks and callbacks for pre-processing and post-processing controls.

docs.title: 5.3 Execution Controls
docs.text: Configure verbosity, caching, memory, planning, and manager settings.

docs.title: 5.4 Operational Signals
docs.text: Capture usage metrics and logs for performance and governance analysis.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Content pipeline crew coordinates research, writing, and QA agents in one run.

docs.title: 06 - Processes: Sequential, Hierarchical, and Hybrid Orchestration
6.1 Sequential Process
docs.text: Tasks run in deterministic order, ideal for linear and auditable workflows.

docs.title: 6.2 Hierarchical Process
docs.text: Manager agent or manager LLM delegates and validates task execution paths.

docs.title: 6.3 Hybrid Control
docs.text: Mix deterministic sequencing with manager-driven adaptation for complex workflows.

docs.title: 6.4 Strategy Selection
docs.text: Choose process mode by risk profile, variability, and throughput needs.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident workflow uses hierarchical manager for adaptive escalation under changing severity.

docs.title: 07 - Tools, Custom Tools, and Execution Safety
7.1 Tool Ecosystem
docs.text: CrewAI supports built-in and custom tools across web, data, automation, and integrations.

docs.title: 7.2 Custom Tooling
docs.text: Create domain-specific tools for internal APIs and operational workflows.

docs.title: 7.3 Safety Controls
docs.text: Enforce input validation, tool scoping, and side-effect constraints.

docs.title: 7.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Apply retries, timeouts, and error classification for stable execution.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Finance tool validates transaction parameters and blocks unsafe transfer operations.

docs.title: 08 - Flows: start, listen, router, and State Modeling
8.1 Flow Model
docs.text: Flows orchestrate event-driven execution across methods, agents, and crews.

docs.title: 8.2 Entry and Routing
docs.text: Use @start, @listen, and @router to control branching and progression.

docs.title: 8.3 State Design
docs.text: Support structured (Pydantic) and unstructured state for workflow context.

docs.title: 8.4 Conditional Patterns
docs.text: Use conjunction logic and router labels for complex branching graphs.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support flow routes billing, technical, or fraud requests to specialized downstream paths.

docs.title: 09 - Flow Persistence, Resumability, and Long-Running Workflows
9.1 Persistence Purpose
docs.text: Persist flow state to survive restarts and continue long-running automations.

docs.title: 9.2 Persistence Scopes
docs.text: Apply @persist at class level or method level based on control needs.

docs.title: 9.3 Resume Strategy
docs.text: Use state IDs and checkpointed data to safely resume interrupted workflows.

docs.title: 9.4 Reliability Patterns
docs.text: Combine persistence with retry logic and idempotent side-effect boundaries.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Background onboarding flow resumes after outage without losing user progress.

docs.title: 10 - Memory: Short-Term, Long-Term, and Entity Memory
10.1 Memory Layers
docs.text: CrewAI memory can include short-term context, long-term memory, and entity-focused memory.

docs.title: 10.2 Memory Design
docs.text: Select what to store, when to retrieve, and how to age out stale knowledge.

docs.title: 10.3 Store Configuration
docs.text: Configure memory backends and embedding strategies for retrieval quality.

docs.title: 10.4 Operational Controls
docs.text: Monitor memory query/save events and error conditions through event listeners.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer success agent recalls previous account incidents to personalize recommendations.

docs.title: 11 - Knowledge Sources, RAG, and Context Enrichment
11.1 Knowledge Layer
docs.text: Knowledge sources inject domain context into agent decisions and outputs.

docs.title: 11.2 Source Strategy
docs.text: Combine documents, APIs, and custom knowledge sources for robust grounding.

docs.title: 11.3 Retrieval Quality
docs.text: Tune chunking, embeddings, and filtering for high-precision knowledge recall.

docs.title: 11.4 Observability
docs.text: Track knowledge retrieval events to debug coverage and relevance issues.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Policy assistant grounds responses in approved internal documentation with audit trail.

docs.title: 12 - LLM Integration, Model Selection, and Provider Strategy
12.1 Provider Abstraction
docs.text: CrewAI supports multiple model providers and custom LLM integrations.

docs.title: 12.2 Model Selection
docs.text: Match models to task complexity, latency targets, and cost constraints.

docs.title: 12.3 Function Calling Strategy
docs.text: Use dedicated function-calling models where tool reliability is critical.

docs.title: 12.4 Fallback Architecture
docs.text: Design provider fallback and graceful degradation for resilience.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Operations assistant routes summarization to low-cost model and planning to stronger model.

docs.title: 13.1 Reasoning Controls
docs.text: Enable reasoning behaviors for reflective planning before execution.

docs.title: 13.2 Planning Layer
docs.text: Planning can generate iteration-level plans appended to tasks.

docs.title: 13.3 Collaboration Model
docs.text: Agents collaborate through shared task context and crew process controls.

docs.title: 13.4 Delegation Safety
docs.text: Bound delegation with role scope and validation checkpoints.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Manager agent delegates market research subtasks and validates outputs before synthesis.

docs.title: 14.1 Event System
docs.text: CrewAI event bus emits crew, agent, task, tool, memory, flow, and LLM events.

docs.title: 14.2 Custom Listeners
docs.text: Implement BaseEventListener handlers for monitoring and integrations.

docs.title: 14.3 Tracing and Telemetry
docs.text: Use tracing integrations and prompt tracking for debugging and auditability.

docs.title: 14.4 Operational Best Practices
docs.text: Keep handlers lightweight, resilient, and selective to avoid runtime overhead.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Custom listener sends task-failure events to incident response system in real time.

docs.title: 15 - Labs, Projects, Glossary, and Operational Checklists
Lab 1
docs.text: Build a YAML-configured crew with two agents and three tasks.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Create a flow using @start, @listen, and @router with structured state.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Add memory and knowledge sources, then monitor retrieval with event listeners.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Implement guardrails plus human review for a regulated approval workflow.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• agent
  • task
  • crew
  • flow
  • guardrail

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• task contracts validated
  • process strategy documented
  • observability listeners active
  • rollback and incident runbooks prepared

docs.title: 16 - Advanced Agent Architecture and Role Engineering
16.1 Role Design
docs.text: Define narrow, high-signal roles with explicit goals and constraints.

docs.title: 16.2 Capability Partitioning
docs.text: Separate planning, execution, and validation responsibilities across agents.

docs.title: 16.3 Behavioral Controls
docs.text: Tune reasoning, iteration limits, and tool scopes for predictable outcomes.

docs.title: 16.4 Persona Governance
docs.text: Version and test role/backstory prompts as controlled artifacts.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Risk-analysis crew uses separate investigator and auditor agents to reduce false positives.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Task Orchestration, Guardrail Chains, and HITL
17.1 Task Graph Design
docs.text: Compose dependency-aware tasks with explicit input and output contracts.

docs.title: 17.2 Guardrail Chains
docs.text: Use multi-stage validation pipelines for format, policy, and factual integrity.

docs.title: 17.3 HITL Gating
docs.text: Insert human checkpoints at high-risk transitions and side-effect boundaries.

docs.title: 17.4 Recovery Logic
docs.text: Route failed guardrails to correction tasks and re-validation loops.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Legal-drafting task chain blocks publication until reviewer approval and schema compliance.

docs.title: 18 - Advanced Crew Orchestration, Manager Agents, and Async Execution
18.1 Manager-Driven Control
docs.text: Hierarchical crews use manager agents or manager LLMs for dynamic delegation.

docs.title: 18.2 Async Execution
docs.text: Use async kickoff patterns for high-throughput, concurrent workloads.

docs.title: 18.3 Coordination Contracts
docs.text: Define handoff metadata, confidence signals, and acceptance criteria.

docs.title: 18.4 Throughput Tuning
docs.text: Bound concurrency to protect downstream tools and model endpoints.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident response crew triages multiple alerts in parallel while manager prioritizes escalations.

docs.title: 19 - Advanced Flows: Event-Driven Branching and Compositions
19.1 Composition Patterns
docs.text: Compose multiple flows for reusable and modular automation architecture.

docs.title: 19.2 Branching Strategy
docs.text: Use router labels and conditional listeners for complex decision trees.

docs.title: 19.3 Stateful Coordination
docs.text: Maintain deterministic state transitions with explicit event semantics.

docs.title: 19.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Protect branching logic with fallback routes and timeout safeguards.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer lifecycle flow branches onboarding, renewal, or churn-prevention paths by signals.

docs.title: 20 - Production Architecture, Deployment, and Scaling with CrewAI
20.1 Reference Topology
docs.text: API gateway -> flow runtime -> crews -> tools/integrations -> observability and storage.

docs.title: 20.2 Deployment Models
docs.text: Choose managed, self-hosted, or hybrid deployment based on compliance and control needs.

docs.title: 20.3 Scaling Patterns
docs.text: Scale by queue depth, flow concurrency, and tool dependency bottlenecks.

docs.title: 20.4 Operational Discipline
docs.text: Use canary releases, feature flags, and rollback automation.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Automation platform scales flows during end-of-month spikes without SLA violations.

docs.title: 21 - Security, Privacy, and Governance in Agentic Systems
21.1 Security Boundaries
docs.text: Protect agent runtime, tools, secrets, and external integrations.

docs.title: 21.2 Privacy Controls
docs.text: Apply PII minimization, masking, retention policies, and tenant isolation.

docs.title: 21.3 Governance Framework
docs.text: Define ownership for prompts, tools, flows, and production change approvals.

docs.title: 21.4 Auditability
docs.text: Capture agent decisions, task outcomes, and human approvals for compliance.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Healthcare workflow masks PHI before tool calls and logs all approval checkpoints.

docs.title: 22 - Testing, Evaluation, and Regression Quality Gates
22.1 Test Pyramid
docs.text: Combine unit tests for tools, integration tests for crews, and scenario tests for flows.

docs.title: 22.2 Evaluation Metrics
docs.text: Track correctness, task completion rate, latency, cost, and policy compliance.

docs.title: 22.3 Regression Harness
docs.text: Run fixed benchmark suites before each deployment.

docs.title: 22.4 Release Gates
docs.text: Block rollout when quality thresholds or safety requirements regress.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Release is stopped after regression suite detects drop in factual consistency.

docs.title: 23 - Training Workflows and Continuous Improvement Loops
23.1 Training Objective
docs.text: Improve crew behavior from historical runs, feedback, and error analysis.

docs.title: 23.2 Dataset Strategy
docs.text: Curate domain scenarios, expected outputs, and failure examples.

docs.title: 23.3 Iterative Loop
docs.text: Train -> evaluate -> deploy canary -> observe -> retrain.

docs.title: 23.4 Governance Controls
docs.text: Version training data and approvals for reproducibility.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support automation retraining reduces escalation errors over weekly improvement cycles.

docs.title: 24 - MCP Integration in CrewAI: DSL and Transport Patterns
24.1 MCP Role
docs.text: MCP standardizes access to external tools/resources used by crews.

docs.title: 24.2 Integration Methods
docs.text: Use MCP servers as tools through declarative and code-first patterns.

docs.title: 24.3 Transport Options
docs.text: Support local and remote MCP transports depending on environment needs.

docs.title: 24.4 Safety Controls
docs.text: Apply scoped permissions and capability allowlists for MCP-connected tools.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Crew uses MCP server to query CRM and policy resources with consistent contracts.

docs.title: 25.1 Multi-Server Strategy
docs.text: Integrate multiple MCP servers for domain-specific capabilities.

docs.title: 25.2 Routing and Prioritization
docs.text: Route calls by latency, trust level, and capability coverage.

docs.title: 25.3 Security Hardening
docs.text: Enforce auth boundaries, output validation, and audit traces across servers.

docs.title: 25.4 Failure Isolation
docs.text: Isolate server failures to prevent cascading impact on full crew execution.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Finance automation routes accounting and policy queries to separate hardened MCP endpoints.

docs.title: 26 - Enterprise Triggers, Automations, and External Integrations
26.1 Trigger Sources
docs.text: Start flows from APIs, schedules, events, and message queues.

docs.title: 26.2 Integration Architecture
docs.text: Connect enterprise systems such as CRM, ERP, ticketing, and data platforms.

docs.title: 26.3 Automation Patterns
docs.text: Use event-driven orchestration for continuous business workflows.

docs.title: 26.4 Operational Controls
docs.text: Add deduplication, idempotency, and replay-safe handlers.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Order exception flow is triggered from ERP events and resolves via multi-agent collaboration.

docs.title: 27 - Performance Tuning, Cost Optimization, and Capacity Planning
27.1 Performance Levers
docs.text: Tune model choice, tool parallelism, and flow branching complexity.

docs.title: 27.2 Cost Controls
docs.text: Set token budgets, response limits, and provider routing strategies.

docs.title: 27.3 Capacity Planning
docs.text: Forecast throughput from event rates, queue depth, and latency SLOs.

docs.title: 27.4 Operational Metrics
docs.text: Track p95 latency, cost per workflow, and failure-domain concentration.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Operations team cuts workflow cost by routing low-risk tasks to cheaper model tier.

docs.title: 28.1 Migration Drivers
docs.text: Framework updates, provider changes, and process improvements require staged migration.

docs.title: 28.2 Compatibility Matrix
docs.text: Validate agents, tasks, tools, and flows across framework versions.

docs.title: 28.3 Safe Rollout
docs.text: Use dual-run validation, canaries, and rollback plans for zero-downtime change.

docs.title: 28.4 Contract Stability
docs.text: Version prompts, schemas, and guardrails as explicit release artifacts.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team upgrades runtime with parallel canary traffic before full production cutover.

docs.title: 29 - Enterprise Reference Architectures and Domain Blueprints
29.1 Reference Blueprint
docs.text: Ingress -> orchestration flows -> crews -> tools/data -> governance and observability.

docs.title: 29.2 Domain Templates
docs.text: Support, finance, healthcare, and operations patterns share core architecture with domain controls.

docs.title: 29.3 Control Plane Design
docs.text: Centralize policy, monitoring, and release governance for all agentic workloads.

docs.title: 29.4 Integration Guidelines
docs.text: Standardize interface contracts for predictable enterprise scaling.

docs.title: 29.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Global enterprise runs shared agent platform with domain-specific crew packs.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: Design a Production CrewAI Platform
30.1 Capstone Objective
docs.text: Design a full production platform using CrewAI agents, crews, flows, and governance controls.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text: Architecture diagram, process strategy, memory/knowledge plan, evaluation framework, and SLO model.

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text: Score reliability, safety, cost-efficiency, and operational maturity.

docs.title: 30.4 Operational Scenario
docs.text: Include failure simulation, rollback playbook, and incident communication plan.

docs.title: 30.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Capstone solution automates customer operations with audited approvals and event-driven escalation.

docs.title: CrewAI for Agentic AI

docs.title: 01 - Program Overview and Agentic Design Pattern Mindset
1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build complete practical mastery of reusable design patterns for agentic AI systems.

docs.title: 1.2 Why Patterns
docs.text: Patterns convert ad-hoc agent behavior into repeatable, auditable architecture decisions.

docs.title: 1.3 Pattern Axes
docs.text: Control flow, memory, tooling, governance, reliability, and operations.

docs.title: 1.4 Learning Strategy
docs.text: Foundations establish pattern vocabulary; advanced chapters operationalize at enterprise scale.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support platform standardizes planning and approval patterns across all agent teams.

docs.title: 02 - System Decomposition, Roles, Contracts, and Boundaries
2.1 Role Decomposition
docs.text: Separate planner, executor, validator, and coordinator responsibilities.

docs.title: 2.2 Contract Design
docs.text: Define strict input/output contracts for each role and component.

docs.title: 2.3 Boundary Controls
docs.text: Enforce data, tool, and decision boundaries to limit failure blast radius.

docs.title: 2.4 Ownership Model
docs.text: Assign ownership for each role contract and interface lifecycle.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Finance assistant separates quote generation from approval and settlement execution.

docs.title: 3.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Split task decomposition from action execution for clarity and control.

docs.title: 3.2 Planner Responsibilities
docs.text: Generate ordered action plans with confidence and dependency metadata.

docs.title: 3.3 Executor Responsibilities
docs.text: Run actions deterministically and report verifiable outcomes.

docs.title: 3.4 Tradeoffs
docs.text: Improves auditability but adds orchestration complexity.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident planner sequences diagnostics while executors run probes and collect evidence.

docs.title: 4.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Use a central supervisor to assign and coordinate specialized workers.

docs.title: 4.2 Supervisor Role
docs.text: Route tasks, enforce policy, and synthesize final outputs.

docs.title: 4.3 Worker Role
docs.text: Perform domain-specific tasks with bounded autonomy.

docs.title: 4.4 Governance Benefit
docs.text: Centralized control simplifies policy and approval enforcement.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support supervisor delegates billing, technical, and policy tasks to worker agents.

docs.title: 5.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Route incoming requests to the most suitable specialist path.

docs.title: 5.2 Router Logic
docs.text: Use intent, risk, and context features for dispatch decisions.

docs.title: 5.3 Specialist Design
docs.text: Specialists optimize for narrow domains with high precision.

docs.title: 5.4 Failure Strategy
docs.text: Fallback to generalist paths when classification confidence is low.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Router sends compliance queries to policy agent and troubleshooting to technical agent.

docs.title: 6.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Improve output quality through structured critique before final response.

docs.title: 6.2 Critic Role
docs.text: Evaluate factuality, completeness, and policy compliance.

docs.title: 6.3 Reflection Loop
docs.text: Revise outputs based on critique with bounded iteration depth.

docs.title: 6.4 Cost Controls
docs.text: Limit loop count and use selective triggering for high-risk tasks only.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Contract summary is revised after critic flags missing liability clause.

docs.title: 07 - Tool Calling and Action Execution Pattern
7.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Convert model decisions into validated, auditable external actions.

docs.title: 7.2 Contract Requirements
docs.text: Tools require strict schema, side-effect classification, and timeout policies.

docs.title: 7.3 Execution Guardrails
docs.text: Apply confirmation gates for high-impact or irreversible operations.

docs.title: 7.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Use retries, idempotency keys, and compensation handlers.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Payment tool requires dual confirmation and idempotency token before transfer.

docs.title: 8.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Ground model outputs on retrieved evidence to improve factual reliability.

docs.title: 8.2 Core Stages
docs.text: Ingest -> index -> retrieve -> rerank -> synthesize -> cite.

docs.title: 8.3 Quality Controls
docs.text: Use citation coverage and faithfulness checks before response release.

docs.title: 8.4 Failure Handling
docs.text: Trigger re-retrieval when evidence confidence is insufficient.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Policy assistant refuses answer when retrieval sources are stale or conflicting.

docs.title: 9.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Balance personalization with safe, bounded memory retention.

docs.title: 9.2 Memory Layers
docs.text: Short-term session context plus long-term user/task memory.

docs.title: 9.3 Lifecycle Policies
docs.text: Capture, summarize, expire, and delete memory artifacts by rule.

docs.title: 9.4 Safety Boundaries
docs.text: Prevent sensitive cross-user memory leakage with strict isolation.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Assistant remembers customer preferences while expiring outdated ticket history.

docs.title: 10.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Insert human judgment before high-risk actions.

docs.title: 10.2 Trigger Conditions
docs.text: Use confidence thresholds, policy flags, and action criticality.

docs.title: 10.3 Approval Workflow
docs.text: Pause, present context, collect decision, and resume safely.

docs.title: 10.4 Audit Requirements
docs.text: Log approver identity, decision rationale, and timestamps.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Procurement workflow pauses before contract-signing action for legal approval.

docs.title: 11.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Decouple agent tasks using event streams and asynchronous processing.

docs.title: 11.2 Event Contracts
docs.text: Define stable event schemas and routing topics.

docs.title: 11.3 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Use retries, dead-letter queues, and replay-safe consumers.

docs.title: 11.4 Throughput Benefits
docs.text: Asynchrony improves scalability for long-running workflows.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Ticket events trigger triage agents without blocking user-facing interactions.

docs.title: 12 - State Machine and Graph Orchestration Pattern
12.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Model workflow states explicitly for deterministic control and replayability.

docs.title: 12.2 State Design
docs.text: Define valid transitions, terminal conditions, and error boundaries.

docs.title: 12.3 Graph Benefits
docs.text: Supports branching, loops, subgraphs, and resumability.

docs.title: 12.4 Debugging Advantage
docs.text: Explicit state transitions simplify incident root-cause analysis.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Onboarding flow resumes from last approved state after service interruption.

docs.title: 13 - Reliability, Retry, Idempotency, and Compensation Pattern
13.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Maintain correctness under partial failures and retries.

docs.title: 13.2 Retry Design
docs.text: Use bounded backoff with error-type-aware retry policies.

docs.title: 13.3 Idempotency
docs.text: Ensure repeated execution does not duplicate side effects.

docs.title: 13.4 Compensation
docs.text: Define undo workflows for partially completed multi-step actions.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Order workflow retries inventory check but avoids duplicate reservation.

docs.title: 14 - Observability, Evaluation, and Quality Gate Pattern
14.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Continuously measure and enforce quality before release.

docs.title: 14.2 Observability Signals
docs.text: Collect traces, latency, tool errors, and policy violation events.

docs.title: 14.3 Evaluation Layer
docs.text: Measure faithfulness, relevance, safety, and task success.

docs.title: 14.4 Release Gates
docs.text: Block deployment when quality metrics regress below threshold.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Canary rollout is halted after evaluator detects increased hallucination rate.

docs.title: 15 - Labs, Projects, Glossary, and Operational Checklists
Lab 1
docs.text: Implement planner-executor and supervisor-worker workflows for same business case.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Add reflection and approval-gate patterns to high-risk action flow.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Build event-driven workflow with checkpoints and replay-safe recovery.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Set up evaluation gates with synthetic regression scenarios.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• planner-executor
  • supervisor-worker
  • reducer
  • idempotency
  • quality gate

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• contracts validated
  • safety gates active
  • observability coverage complete
  • incident runbooks tested

docs.title: 16.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Organize agents in layered command structures for large-scale workflows.

docs.title: 16.2 Hierarchy Design
docs.text: Top-level strategy agents coordinate mid-level planners and execution workers.

docs.title: 16.3 Escalation Paths
docs.text: Define clear escalation routes for ambiguous or high-risk outcomes.

docs.title: 16.4 Tradeoffs
docs.text: Improves governance but adds coordination overhead.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Enterprise operations uses tiered incident hierarchy from triage to executive escalation.

docs.title: 17.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Enable decentralized agent collaboration for exploratory problem spaces.

docs.title: 17.2 Swarm Mechanics
docs.text: Agents share partial solutions and iterate toward consensus or best candidate.

docs.title: 17.3 Market-Based Control
docs.text: Use scoring, bidding, or utility signals to allocate tasks.

docs.title: 17.4 Governance Needs
docs.text: Require strong safeguards to prevent unbounded collaboration loops.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Research swarm explores multiple hypotheses and selects best-evidence synthesis.

docs.title: 18 - Blackboard and Shared Knowledge Space Pattern
18.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Use a shared workspace where agents post intermediate findings.

docs.title: 18.2 Blackboard Model
docs.text: Agents read/write standardized artifacts to coordinate asynchronously.

docs.title: 18.3 Consistency Controls
docs.text: Apply versioning, conflict resolution, and provenance tracking.

docs.title: 18.4 Quality Benefits
docs.text: Encourages explainable collaboration and reproducible outcomes.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Investigation agents share evidence graph in blackboard before final risk decision.

docs.title: 19 - Multi-Stage RAG and Verifier Loop Pattern
19.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Improve answer reliability with staged retrieval and verification loops.

docs.title: 19.2 Stage Design
docs.text: Candidate retrieval -> reranking -> synthesis -> verification.

docs.title: 19.3 Verifier Role
docs.text: Independent verifier checks factual grounding and citation adequacy.

docs.title: 19.4 Control Policies
docs.text: Trigger re-retrieval when verifier confidence is below threshold.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Compliance assistant re-runs retrieval until citations cover all regulatory claims.

docs.title: 20 - Protocol-First Interoperability: MCP and A2A Pattern
20.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Standardize interoperability through shared protocol contracts.

docs.title: 20.2 MCP Role
docs.text: MCP provides portable tool/resource integration across hosts.

docs.title: 20.3 A2A Role
docs.text: A2A enables agent-to-agent task delegation across systems.

docs.title: 20.4 Security Model
docs.text: Use scoped permissions, identity checks, and audit logging.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Coordinator delegates vendor analysis via A2A and uses MCP for internal data tools.

docs.title: 21 - Zero-Trust Security Pattern for Agentic Systems
21.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Never trust by default; verify identity and policy at every step.

docs.title: 21.2 Control Points
docs.text: Authenticate agents, authorize tools, and validate data flows continuously.

docs.title: 21.3 Segmentation
docs.text: Isolate tenants, data classes, and high-risk execution zones.

docs.title: 21.4 Monitoring
docs.text: Detect anomalous behavior and trigger immediate containment.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Zero-trust policy blocks unauthorized cross-tenant tool access during delegation.

docs.title: 22.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Wrap all side-effecting actions in policy and approval controls.

docs.title: 22.2 Policy Engine
docs.text: Evaluate context, actor, and action risk before execution.

docs.title: 22.3 Enforcement Modes
docs.text: Support allow, deny, transform, or escalate outcomes.

docs.title: 22.4 Audit Requirements
docs.text: Record policy decisions and justifications for governance.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Procurement action is denied when approval chain is incomplete.

docs.title: 23.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Support workflows spanning hours or days with safe resumability.

docs.title: 23.2 Planning Structure
docs.text: Break long goals into milestones with explicit checkpoints.

docs.title: 23.3 Resume Semantics
docs.text: Resume from last verified checkpoint after interruption.

docs.title: 23.4 Failure Recovery
docs.text: Use compensation paths for partially completed milestones.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Onboarding automation resumes from compliance checkpoint after maintenance outage.

docs.title: 24.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Optimize quality-cost balance through dynamic model selection.

docs.title: 24.2 Routing Rules
docs.text: Route by complexity, confidence, latency budget, and compliance tier.

docs.title: 24.3 Escalation Logic
docs.text: Escalate to stronger models only when confidence is low.

docs.title: 24.4 Budget Governance
docs.text: Enforce per-workflow token and spend limits.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support bot handles standard queries with low-cost model and escalates disputes.

docs.title: 25 - Versioning, Canary, and Migration Pattern for Agent Workflows
25.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Evolve workflows safely without breaking production behavior.

docs.title: 25.2 Version Strategy
docs.text: Version prompts, tool schemas, and state contracts independently.

docs.title: 25.3 Canary Rollout
docs.text: Expose new versions to small traffic slices before full release.

docs.title: 25.4 Migration Safety
docs.text: Use dual-run and rollback checkpoints for critical flows.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Workflow v2 rolls back automatically after canary quality regression.

docs.title: 26 - Incident Response and Chaos Engineering Pattern
26.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Build operational resilience through planned failure testing.

docs.title: 26.2 Incident Lifecycle
docs.text: Detect, triage, contain, recover, and review with runbook discipline.

docs.title: 26.3 Chaos Testing
docs.text: Inject controlled failures in tools, models, and dependencies.

docs.title: 26.4 Learning Loop
docs.text: Convert incident findings into test and policy improvements.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Chaos test simulates vector store outage and validates fallback retrieval path.

docs.title: 27.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Protect tenant boundaries across data, memory, and tool execution.

docs.title: 27.2 Isolation Methods
docs.text: Use namespaces, scoped credentials, and policy-based access controls.

docs.title: 27.3 Governance Model
docs.text: Apply tenant-level audit, retention, and policy override workflows.

docs.title: 27.4 Compliance Readiness
docs.text: Map tenant controls to regional and regulatory obligations.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Platform prevents cross-tenant retrieval despite shared infrastructure.

docs.title: 28 - Enterprise Integration and Event Bus Pattern
28.1 Pattern Intent
docs.text: Integrate agent workflows with enterprise systems through event-first architecture.

docs.title: 28.2 Integration Layer
docs.text: Connect CRM, ERP, ticketing, and data platforms with standardized adapters.

docs.title: 28.3 Event Bus Design
docs.text: Define topic taxonomy, contracts, and replay policies.

docs.title: 28.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Apply idempotent consumers and dead-letter handling.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Order exception events trigger agent remediation and status updates in ERP.

docs.title: 29.1 Pattern Catalog
docs.text: Curate reusable patterns by domain and risk profile.

docs.title: 29.2 Domain Mapping
docs.text: Support, finance, healthcare, and operations each map to tailored pattern sets.

docs.title: 29.3 Selection Framework
docs.text: Choose patterns by compliance burden, latency SLO, and task complexity.

docs.title: 29.4 Governance Workflow
docs.text: Review pattern updates with architecture board and operational stakeholders.

docs.title: 29.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Healthcare domain uses strict approval + citation patterns while support uses fast routing patterns.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: Design Pattern System Architecture
30.1 Capstone Goal
docs.text: Design a production agent platform using a coherent set of proven patterns.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text: Architecture map, pattern selection rationale, governance model, and SLO strategy.

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text: Assess reliability, safety, scalability, and operational maturity.

docs.title: 30.4 Failure Simulation
docs.text: Include rollback, incident recovery, and postmortem-driven improvement workflow.

docs.title: 30.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Capstone platform combines planner-executor, HITL gates, and protocol interoperability.

docs.title: Design Patterns for Agentic AI
docs.text: This folder contains a complete Foundations + Advanced documentation set.

docs.title: Open First
docs.text: • INDEX.md

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
1. Foundations Track: chapters 01-15
  2. Advanced Track: chapters 16-30

docs.text:

This course uses one index file with two sections.

docs.title: Foundations Track (01-15)

docs.title: Advanced Track (16-30)
docs.text: Chapter File Coverage 16 16-Hierarchical-Multi-Agent-Organization-Pattern.md Deep hierarchy design 17 17-Swarm-and-Market-Based-Coordination-Pattern.md Decentralized coordination patterns 18 18-Blackboard-and-Shared-Knowledge-Space-Pattern.md Shared workspace architecture 19 19-Multi-Stage-RAG-and-Verifier-Loop-Pattern.md Multi-stage RAG and verification 20 20-Protocol-First-Interoperability-MCP-and-A2A-Pattern.md Protocol interoperability patterns 21 21-Zero-Trust-Security-Pattern-for-Agentic-Systems.md Zero-trust security architecture 22 22-Policy-Governed-Action-Execution-Pattern.md Runtime policy and action governance 23 23-Long-Horizon-Planning-and-Checkpoint-Resume-Pattern.md Long-running workflow resilience 24 24-Cost-Aware-Adaptive-Model-Routing-Pattern.md Cost-quality adaptive routing 25 25-Versioning-Canary-and-Migration-Pattern-for-Agent-Workflows.md Workflow migration and rollout patterns 26 26-Incident-Response-and-Chaos-Engineering-Pattern.md Reliability operations at scale 27 27-Multi-Tenant-Isolation-and-Governance-Pattern.md Tenant-safe architecture patterns 28 28-Enterprise-Integration-and-Event-Bus-Pattern.md Enterprise integration blueprints 29 29-Domain-Reference-Architecture-Pattern-Library.md Domain-specific pattern catalog 30 30-Advanced-Capstone-Design-Pattern-System-Architecture.md End-to-end capstone

docs.title: 01 - Program Overview and Google ADK Ecosystem
1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build complete production-ready expertise in Google Agent Development Kit (ADK).

docs.title: 1.2 ADK Positioning
docs.text: ADK is a modular, model-agnostic framework for building, orchestrating, and deploying AI agents.

docs.title: 1.3 Ecosystem Components
docs.text: Agents, tools, sessions, memory, runtime, deployment, observability, and evaluation.

docs.title: 1.4 Learning Path
docs.text: Foundations -> orchestration -> enterprise controls -> production operations.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer-support system combines multi-agent delegation with tool-backed policy retrieval.

docs.title: 2.1 SDK Landscape
docs.text: ADK supports Python, TypeScript, Go, and Java.

docs.title: 2.2 Installation Basics
docs.text: Install language-specific ADK packages and configure provider credentials.

docs.title: 2.3 Environment Strategy
docs.text: Use isolated environments, secret management, and pinned dependencies.

docs.title: 2.4 Project Bootstrap
docs.text: Create a baseline project with runtime configuration and starter agent definitions.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team shares architecture across Python and Go services with consistent runtime policies.

docs.title: 03 - ADK Technical Overview and Core Architecture
3.1 Core Runtime
docs.text: ADK orchestrates agent execution using structured runtime components.

docs.title: 3.2 Modularity
docs.text: Agents, tools, memory, and callbacks can be composed independently.

docs.title: 3.3 Extensibility
docs.text: Custom integrations plug into standardized interfaces.

docs.title: 3.4 Architecture Tradeoffs
docs.text: Choose deterministic workflow paths or LLM-driven dynamic control.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Operations workflow mixes deterministic pathing with adaptive model decisions.

docs.title: 04 - Agent Types: LLM, Workflow, and Custom Agents
4.1 LLM Agents
docs.text: Handle reasoning, language generation, and tool invocation.

docs.title: 4.2 Workflow Agents
docs.text: Use predefined logic patterns for predictable orchestration.

docs.title: 4.3 Custom Agents
docs.text: Implement specialized behaviors and domain-specific integrations.

docs.title: 4.4 Composition Pattern
docs.text: Complex systems combine all three agent types.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Fraud platform uses workflow manager, LLM analyst, and custom scoring agent together.

docs.title: 05 - Workflow Agents: Sequential, Parallel, and Loop Patterns
5.1 Sequential Pattern
docs.text: Deterministic step-by-step execution for auditable workflows.

docs.title: 5.2 Parallel Pattern
docs.text: Execute independent tasks concurrently to reduce total latency.

docs.title: 5.3 Loop Pattern
docs.text: Iterative workflows for refinement, retries, or bounded convergence.

docs.title: 5.4 Pattern Selection
docs.text: Pick orchestration style based on risk, complexity, and throughput needs.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Claims processing runs checks in parallel, then loops remediation until validation passes.

docs.title: 6.1 Multi-Agent Value
docs.text: Specialized agents improve quality for complex multi-step tasks.

docs.title: 6.2 Delegation Strategy
docs.text: Assign subtasks to domain-specific agents with explicit handoff contracts.

docs.title: 6.3 Collaboration Controls
docs.text: Bound turn counts, role permissions, and escalation rules.

docs.title: 6.4 Failure Handling
docs.text: Fallback paths and coordinator agents prevent deadlock.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Travel assistant delegates pricing, policy, and itinerary generation to specialized agents.

docs.title: 07 - Models for Agents: Gemini and Provider Integration
7.1 Model Abstraction
docs.text: ADK is optimized for Gemini but supports broader model integration patterns.

docs.title: 7.2 Provider Strategy
docs.text: Map task classes to model capabilities and cost constraints.

docs.title: 7.3 Routing Basics
docs.text: Use simple routing rules for quality, latency, and budget balance.

docs.title: 7.4 Resilience
docs.text: Implement fallback provider and retry strategy for outages.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support assistant routes FAQ tasks to low-cost model and escalates disputes.

docs.title: 08 - Tools and Integrations: Function, MCP, and OpenAPI Tools
8.1 Tool Types
docs.text: ADK supports function tools, MCP tools, and OpenAPI-based tools.

docs.title: 8.2 Integration Workflow
docs.text: Register tools, validate schemas, and attach to eligible agents.

docs.title: 8.3 Security Controls
docs.text: Constrain tool permissions and enforce input/output validation.

docs.title: 8.4 Operational Limits
docs.text: Design for latency, side effects, and retry-safe execution.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Finance agent queries billing API through OpenAPI tool and policy database via MCP.

docs.title: 09 - Sessions, State, Memory, and Context Management
9.1 Session Model
docs.text: A session represents one conversation thread with event history.

docs.title: 9.2 Session State
docs.text: Session state stores thread-specific working context.

docs.title: 9.3 Memory Layer
docs.text: Memory enables searchable context across sessions and long-term knowledge.

docs.title: 9.4 Service Options
docs.text: Choose in-memory services for dev and persistent backends for production.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Agent resumes support conversation with prior thread state and user memory.

docs.title: 10 - Callbacks, Events, and Artifacts in ADK
10.1 Callback Model
docs.text: Callbacks allow interception for logging, policy checks, and adaptive control.

docs.title: 10.2 Events
docs.text: Event streams capture execution steps for debugging and observability.

docs.title: 10.3 Artifacts
docs.text: Artifacts manage files and binary outputs produced during agent workflows.

docs.title: 10.4 Governance
docs.text: Track callback decisions and artifact lineage for compliance.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Report-generation flow stores generated PDF artifact and emits traceable events.

docs.title: 11 - Agent Runtime, Web, CLI, API, and Configuration
11.1 Runtime Modes
docs.text: Run agents through web interface, CLI, or API server modes.

docs.title: 11.2 Configuration
docs.text: Use runtime config and agent config YAML for reproducible behavior.

docs.title: 11.3 Resume Behavior
docs.text: Resume long-running or interrupted workflows with stored state.

docs.title: 11.4 Operational Use
docs.text: Choose runtime mode based on dev speed, automation, and integration needs.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Ops team uses CLI for batch runs and API server for product integration.

docs.title: 12 - Deployment: Agent Engine, Cloud Run, and GKE
12.1 Deployment Options
docs.text: Deploy locally, on Vertex AI Agent Engine, Cloud Run, or GKE.

docs.title: 12.2 Topology Choice
docs.text: Select topology by scale target, latency requirements, and governance needs.

docs.title: 12.3 CI/CD Controls
docs.text: Use staged rollout, canary validation, and rollback automation.

docs.title: 12.4 Platform Readiness
docs.text: Prepare secrets, observability, and failure-recovery runbooks.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Production assistant deploys on Cloud Run with autoscaling and policy-aware tool access.

docs.title: 13.1 Core Signals
docs.text: Collect logs, traces, model metrics, and tool performance indicators.

docs.title: 13.2 Telemetry Architecture
docs.text: Correlate agent steps with request and session identifiers.

docs.title: 13.3 Debugging Workflows
docs.text: Trace failures through callbacks, tool calls, and model outputs.

docs.title: 13.4 Alerting
docs.text: Set alerts for latency spikes, failure bursts, and policy violations.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Telemetry reveals failing external tool dependency causing repeated retries.

docs.title: 14.1 Evaluation Strategy
docs.text: Measure final response quality and execution trajectory quality.

docs.title: 14.2 Safety Controls
docs.text: Implement guardrails, approvals, and high-risk action constraints.

docs.title: 14.3 Security Basics
docs.text: Protect credentials, restrict tool permissions, and enforce data boundaries.

docs.title: 14.4 Governance
docs.text: Tie evaluation and safety checks to release gates.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Release pipeline blocks deploy after simulated unsafe tool-call behavior.

docs.title: 15 - Labs, Projects, Glossary, and Operational Checklists
Lab 1
docs.text: Build a multi-tool LLM agent with deterministic termination conditions.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Create a multi-agent team with delegated subtasks and callback monitoring.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Add session persistence and memory retrieval for cross-session continuity.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Deploy to Cloud Run and validate observability and safety gates.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• LLM agent
  • workflow agent
  • session
  • memory
  • callback

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• tool schemas validated
  • safety callbacks active
  • telemetry dashboards configured
  • rollback procedures tested

docs.title: 16 - Advanced LLM Agent Design and Instruction Architecture
16.1 Instruction Layers
docs.text: Design system, task, and runtime instruction layers with clear priority order.

docs.title: 16.2 Behavioral Constraints
docs.text: Enforce role boundaries, tool policies, and escalation thresholds.

docs.title: 16.3 Prompt Lifecycle
docs.text: Version and test agent instructions as governed production artifacts.

docs.title: 16.4 Adaptation Strategy
docs.text: Use context-aware instruction modulation for dynamic workflows.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Regulated support agent adapts instruction strictness based on customer risk tier.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Workflow Orchestration and Hybrid Control Patterns
17.1 Hybrid Orchestration
docs.text: Combine deterministic workflow steps with adaptive LLM-driven branches.

docs.title: 17.2 Control Plane
docs.text: Centralize routing rules, retries, and failure fallback behavior.

docs.title: 17.3 Branch Governance
docs.text: Constrain branch fan-out and enforce completion criteria.

docs.title: 17.4 Reliability
docs.text: Use checkpoints and idempotent transitions for safe recovery.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Claims workflow runs deterministic validation then adaptive remediation planning.

docs.title: 18 - Advanced Multi-Agent Topology and Delegation Governance
18.1 Topology Patterns
docs.text: Use supervisor-worker, mesh, and hub-and-spoke structures by workload complexity.

docs.title: 18.2 Delegation Contracts
docs.text: Define handoff schema, confidence signals, and acceptance criteria.

docs.title: 18.3 Governance Controls
docs.text: Limit delegation depth and enforce domain-based authorization.

docs.title: 18.4 Failure Isolation
docs.text: Contain agent failures to sub-graphs to avoid cascade disruption.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Operations coordinator delegates incident diagnosis and remediation to specialized agents.

docs.title: 19 - Advanced Tool Engineering, Performance, and Action Confirmations
19.1 Tool Contract Design
docs.text: Define strict schemas, failure codes, and side-effect metadata.

docs.title: 19.2 Performance Engineering
docs.text: Benchmark tool latency and add caching where results are reusable.

docs.title: 19.3 Action Confirmations
docs.text: Require explicit confirmation for high-impact tool operations.

docs.title: 19.4 Runtime Safety
docs.text: Use sandboxing and policy filters for external or code-execution tools.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Payment tool requires user confirmation and risk-score check before execution.

docs.title: 20 - MCP and A2A Interoperability Patterns with ADK
20.1 Protocol Roles
docs.text: Use MCP for tool/resource access and A2A for agent-to-agent collaboration.

docs.title: 20.2 ADK Integration
docs.text: Bridge ADK agents to protocol endpoints with adapter-based architecture.

docs.title: 20.3 Security Model
docs.text: Apply scoped credentials, capability allowlists, and audit traces.

docs.title: 20.4 Operational Strategy
docs.text: Track protocol-level latency and compatibility drift.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Coordinator agent delegates to partner agent over A2A and accesses enterprise tools via MCP.

docs.title: 21 - Advanced Context Caching, Compression, and Memory Lifecycle
21.1 Context Budgeting
docs.text: Prioritize high-signal context under token and latency constraints.

docs.title: 21.2 Compression Techniques
docs.text: Use semantic summarization, deduplication, and salience scoring.

docs.title: 21.3 Cache Strategy
docs.text: Cache retrieval/tool outputs with freshness and invalidation policies.

docs.title: 21.4 Memory Lifecycle
docs.text: Define capture, update, archival, and deletion workflows.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Service agent compresses long ticket history while preserving critical compliance facts.

docs.title: 22 - Runtime Event Loop, Concurrency, and Resumability at Scale
22.1 Event Loop Internals
docs.text: Runtime loop coordinates agent turns, tool invocations, and callback chains.

docs.title: 22.2 Concurrency Model
docs.text: Parallelize safe branches while protecting shared state boundaries.

docs.title: 22.3 Resumability
docs.text: Checkpoint critical transitions for replay-safe recovery after failures.

docs.title: 22.4 Throughput Controls
docs.text: Use backpressure, queue management, and timeout guardrails.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: High-volume support runtime resumes interrupted sessions after transient infrastructure faults.

docs.title: 23 - Production Deployment Reference Architectures and Operations
23.1 Reference Topology
docs.text: Ingress -> runtime/API layer -> orchestration workers -> tool services -> observability plane.

docs.title: 23.2 Platform Options
docs.text: Deploy on Agent Engine, Cloud Run, GKE, or hybrid combinations.

docs.title: 23.3 Release Engineering
docs.text: Adopt canary rollout, feature flags, and automated rollback.

docs.title: 23.4 Operability
docs.text: Prepare runbooks for model outages, tool failures, and policy incidents.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Enterprise rollout uses staged regional deployment with automatic rollback thresholds.

docs.title: 24 - Performance, Cost Optimization, and Capacity Planning
24.1 Performance Model
docs.text: Measure model latency, tool latency, orchestration overhead, and queue wait time.

docs.title: 24.2 Cost Controls
docs.text: Apply model routing, token caps, and adaptive context reduction.

docs.title: 24.3 Capacity Planning
docs.text: Forecast concurrency, compute, and storage from demand profiles.

docs.title: 24.4 Optimization Loop
docs.text: Continuously tune settings from observability feedback.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team halves inference cost by routing low-risk tasks to lower-cost models.

docs.title: 25 - Enterprise Security, IAM, Secrets, and Compliance
25.1 IAM Strategy
docs.text: Apply least-privilege roles to agents, runtime services, and tool connectors.

docs.title: 25.2 Secret Management
docs.text: Store and rotate credentials using managed secret systems.

docs.title: 25.3 Compliance Controls
docs.text: Implement retention, data minimization, and auditable approval policies.

docs.title: 25.4 Continuous Assurance
docs.text: Run policy validation in CI and runtime monitoring.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Financial assistant enforces role-bound tool access and full audit logging.

docs.title: 26 - Testing, Evaluation, Benchmarking, and Regression Gates
26.1 Test Pyramid
docs.text: Unit-test tools, integration-test workflows, and scenario-test full agent systems.

docs.title: 26.2 Evaluation Metrics
docs.text: Track response quality, trajectory quality, safety, latency, and cost.

docs.title: 26.3 Benchmark Harness
docs.text: Use standardized evaluation datasets and replayable test runs.

docs.title: 26.4 Release Gates
docs.text: Block production release when quality or safety regresses.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Regression suite catches policy non-compliance after prompt update.

docs.title: 27 - Advanced Observability, Incident Response, and SLO Management
27.1 Observability Depth
docs.text: Instrument every stage from request ingress to tool side effects.

docs.title: 27.2 Incident Workflows
docs.text: Define triage, containment, and recovery playbooks by failure type.

docs.title: 27.3 SLO Framework
docs.text: Set and monitor SLOs for availability, latency, and safety outcomes.

docs.title: 27.4 Postmortem Discipline
docs.text: Capture root cause and preventive controls for each major incident.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: On-call team isolates faulty tool integration and restores SLA through targeted rollback.

docs.title: 28 - Visual Builder, API Server, and Agent Config Governance
28.1 Visual Builder Workflow
docs.text: Use visual tooling for rapid prototyping and agent graph exploration.

docs.title: 28.2 API Server Governance
docs.text: Standardize runtime endpoints and request contracts for enterprise consumption.

docs.title: 28.3 Config Management
docs.text: Version agent_config and runtime settings as controlled deployment artifacts.

docs.title: 28.4 Change Control
docs.text: Apply review and approval workflow for configuration updates.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team promotes visual prototype to API runtime with versioned config and approval gate.

docs.title: 29 - Migration, Versioning, and Multi-SDK Compatibility Strategy
29.1 Version Management
docs.text: Track breaking and non-breaking changes across ADK releases.

docs.title: 29.2 Multi-SDK Alignment
docs.text: Ensure behavior parity across Python, TypeScript, Go, and Java implementations.

docs.title: 29.3 Migration Playbook
docs.text: Use staged rollout, compatibility matrix testing, and rollback checkpoints.

docs.title: 29.4 Risk Mitigation
docs.text: Freeze high-risk changes during migration windows.

docs.title: 29.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Platform team migrates runtime config format with dual-version support in production.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: Design a Production Google ADK Platform
30.1 Capstone Goal
docs.text: Design a full production ADK platform with governance, reliability, and scale controls.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text: Architecture diagram, agent topology, tool governance plan, evaluation framework, and SLOs.

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text: Assess reliability, security, cost-efficiency, and operational maturity.

docs.title: 30.4 Failure Simulation
docs.text: Include outage scenario, rollback process, and incident communication plan.

docs.title: 30.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Capstone platform automates enterprise support with auditable approvals and resilient runtime behavior.

docs.title: 1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build complete practical and production-ready expertise in LangChain for agentic AI applications.

docs.title: 1.2 LangChain in the Stack
docs.text: LangChain provides high-level agent abstractions built on top of LangGraph runtime capabilities.

docs.title: 1.3 Current Direction
docs.text: LangChain v1 centers on create_agent, standardized content blocks, and streamlined namespaces.

docs.title: 1.4 Ecosystem Components
docs.text: Models, messages, tools, memory, middleware, retrieval, guardrails, and LangSmith operations.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support assistant routes customer queries through model reasoning and tool calls in one runtime loop.

docs.title: 2.1 Core Packages
docs.text: Install langchain plus provider-specific packages such as langchain-openai or langchain-anthropic.

docs.title: 2.2 Environment Design
docs.text: Use per-project virtual environments, pinned dependencies, and secure API key management.

docs.title: 2.3 Dev Workflow
docs.text: Separate local prototyping, staging validation, and production deployment configurations.

docs.title: 2.4 Configuration Hygiene
docs.text: Centralize model IDs, runtime limits, and tracing flags through environment-driven settings.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: A team runs the same agent code in dev and prod by swapping environment-specific model settings.

docs.title: 03 - create_agent Core Architecture and Execution Loop
3.1 create_agent Basics
docs.text: create_agent is the standard LangChain v1 interface for building tool-using agents.

docs.title: 3.2 Agent Loop
docs.text: Core loop: model call -> tool selection/execution -> repeated reasoning -> final response.

docs.title: 3.3 Stop Conditions
docs.text: Agents stop on final answer, iteration limit, or explicit middleware termination.

docs.title: 3.4 Runtime Foundation
docs.text: LangChain agents run on graph-based runtime behavior inherited from LangGraph.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Research agent iteratively calls search and summarization tools before returning a final brief.

docs.title: 4.1 Model Abstraction
docs.text: LangChain standardizes model interfaces across different providers.

docs.title: 4.2 Static vs Dynamic Selection
docs.text: Use static models for simple workloads and dynamic routing for complex cost-quality tradeoffs.

docs.title: 4.3 Parameter Management
docs.text: Tune temperature, token limits, timeouts, and retries based on task profile.

docs.title: 4.4 Provider Strategy
docs.text: Maintain fallback providers and compatibility mappings for reliability.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Agent routes short factual queries to low-cost model and escalation tasks to higher-capability model.

docs.title: 05 - Messages, Content Blocks, and Conversation State
5.1 Message Model
docs.text: Conversation is represented as role-based message sequences in agent state.

docs.title: 5.2 Content Blocks
docs.text: Standardized content blocks unify provider-specific output structures.

docs.title: 5.3 State Management
docs.text: Message history drives context continuity and tool selection quality.

docs.title: 5.4 State Boundaries
docs.text: Design truncation and summarization policies to control context growth.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Billing assistant preserves latest account context while summarizing older conversation turns.

docs.title: 06 - Prompting and Context Engineering with LangChain
6.1 System Prompt Design
docs.text: Define role, constraints, and output expectations at the agent level.

docs.title: 6.2 Context Assembly
docs.text: Combine user intent, memory, retrieved evidence, and tool outputs into coherent prompts.

docs.title: 6.3 Context Engineering Patterns
docs.text: Use guardrails, structured instructions, and selective evidence injection.

docs.title: 6.4 Failure Mitigation
docs.text: Handle ambiguous queries with clarifying prompts and staged reasoning.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Compliance agent injects policy snippets and strict response schema before answer generation.

docs.title: 07 - Tools Design, ToolRuntime, and Execution Safety
7.1 Tool Contracts
docs.text: Tools are callable functions with explicit inputs, outputs, and behavior constraints.

docs.title: 7.2 ToolRuntime Access
docs.text: ToolRuntime provides state, store, context, and stream writer access inside tools.

docs.title: 7.3 Safety Controls
docs.text: Validate arguments, enforce authorization, and limit side-effectful operations.

docs.title: 7.4 Error Handling
docs.text: Use deterministic tool error responses and retry policies where appropriate.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Payment tool requires validated account scope and explicit approval before transfer execution.

docs.title: 08 - Agent Planning, Reasoning, and Stop Conditions
8.1 Planning Behavior
docs.text: Agents decompose tasks into iterative model reasoning and tool action steps.

docs.title: 8.2 Control Parameters
docs.text: Set iteration caps, timeout budgets, and fallback flows.

docs.title: 8.3 Determinism vs Flexibility
docs.text: Balance autonomous exploration with strict workflow constraints.

docs.title: 8.4 Termination Logic
docs.text: Stop safely when goals are met or uncertainty exceeds policy threshold.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Procurement agent stops early and requests approval when quote confidence is insufficient.

docs.title: 9.1 Thread-Scoped Memory
docs.text: Short-term memory tracks active conversation state within a thread.

docs.title: 9.2 Custom State Schemas
docs.text: Extend default state to store domain-specific fields for richer reasoning.

docs.title: 9.3 State via Middleware
docs.text: Use middleware for standardized state transformations and consistency checks.

docs.title: 9.4 Memory Quality
docs.text: Prevent drift through selective retention and summarization.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Travel agent tracks user preferences and current itinerary state during session planning.

docs.title: 10.1 Persistent Memory Model
docs.text: Long-term memory stores user or application data across sessions.

docs.title: 10.2 Store Patterns
docs.text: Use namespace/key strategies for tenant-safe and queryable memory access.

docs.title: 10.3 Operational Stores
docs.text: Prefer persistent backends such as Postgres-backed stores for production.

docs.title: 10.4 Governance Controls
docs.text: Apply retention, deletion, and access policies to stored memory artifacts.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer-success agent recalls account history from previous sessions for personalized support.

docs.title: 11.1 Retrieval Purpose
docs.text: Retrieval adds external, current knowledge to overcome model context and freshness limits.

docs.title: 11.2 RAG Architectures
docs.text: 2-step RAG, agentic RAG, and hybrid RAG each balance control and flexibility differently.

docs.title: 11.3 Building Blocks
docs.text: Loaders, splitters, embeddings, vector stores, retrievers, and reranking pipelines.

docs.title: 11.4 Production Criteria
docs.text: Evaluate recall, citation quality, latency, and answer faithfulness.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Internal knowledge agent switches between direct answer and retrieval-driven response by intent.

docs.title: 12.1 Structured Output Need
docs.text: Schema-constrained output improves reliability for downstream systems.

docs.title: 12.2 Strategy Modes
docs.text: Use provider-native structured output when available; fallback to tool-based strategies otherwise.

docs.title: 12.3 Validation Pipeline
docs.text: Validate output types, required fields, and semantic constraints before execution.

docs.title: 12.4 Failure Recovery
docs.text: Retry with corrective prompts or fallback schemas on validation failure.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Lead-qualification agent outputs strict JSON schema for CRM ingestion without manual cleanup.

docs.title: 13.1 Middleware Hooks
docs.text: Middleware can intercept model calls, tool selection, and output handling steps.

docs.title: 13.2 Guardrails
docs.text: Apply rate limits, PII detection, content policy checks, and response constraints.

docs.title: 13.3 Reliability Enhancements
docs.text: Implement retries, fallbacks, and early termination rules.

docs.title: 13.4 Policy Enforcement
docs.text: Centralize governance rules through reusable middleware modules.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Healthcare agent redacts PII and blocks unsafe medication advice before final response.

docs.title: 14.1 Human-in-the-Loop Patterns
docs.text: Insert approval checkpoints for high-risk or high-impact actions.

docs.title: 14.2 Runtime Controls
docs.text: Control execution with interrupts, timeout boundaries, and context-aware gating.

docs.title: 14.3 Streaming UX
docs.text: Stream intermediate events and tool progress for responsive user experiences.

docs.title: 14.4 Operational Safety
docs.text: Escalate unresolved ambiguity to humans instead of forcing autonomous decisions.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Finance agent pauses before wire-transfer action and resumes after human approval.

docs.title: 15 - Labs, Projects, Glossary, and Operational Checklists
Lab 1
docs.text: Build baseline create_agent with one model and two tools.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Add short-term memory, long-term store, and policy middleware.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Implement agentic RAG with retrieval tool and structured output.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Add human approval checkpoints and real-time tool streaming updates.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• create_agent
  • ToolRuntime
  • middleware
  • structured output
  • agentic RAG

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• tool contracts validated
  • guardrails active
  • tracing and evaluation configured
  • rollback strategy documented

docs.title: 16 - Dynamic Model Routing, Cost, and Latency Optimization
16.1 Routing Strategy
docs.text: Select models dynamically by task complexity, risk, and latency requirements.

docs.title: 16.2 Cost Controls
docs.text: Use budget-aware routing, token caps, and fallback model tiers.

docs.title: 16.3 Latency Engineering
docs.text: Combine fast-path models with escalation paths for difficult tasks.

docs.title: 16.4 Governance Layer
docs.text: Enforce routing rules through middleware for consistency.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer agent handles simple FAQs cheaply and escalates complex claims to stronger models.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Tool Orchestration, Parallelism, and Recovery
17.1 Orchestration Patterns
docs.text: Use sequential, parallel, and conditional tool workflows for complex objectives.

docs.title: 17.2 Parallel Execution
docs.text: Run independent tools concurrently to reduce end-to-end latency.

docs.title: 17.3 Recovery Patterns
docs.text: Implement retries, circuit breakers, and fallback tools for resilience.

docs.title: 17.4 Idempotency Design
docs.text: Prevent duplicate side effects on tool re-execution.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Operations agent queries monitoring and ticket systems in parallel before generating incident summary.

docs.title: 18 - Multi-Agent Architectures with LangChain and LangGraph
18.1 Multi-Agent Need
docs.text: Specialized agents improve quality for complex multi-domain tasks.

docs.title: 18.2 Coordination Architectures
docs.text: Apply supervisor-worker, committee, and planner-executor designs.

docs.title: 18.3 LangChain and LangGraph
docs.text: Use LangChain abstractions for fast development and LangGraph patterns for controlled orchestration.

docs.title: 18.4 Failure Boundaries
docs.text: Isolate agent roles and define explicit handoff contracts.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Sales assistant delegates pricing, legal, and competitor analysis to specialist sub-agents.

docs.title: 19 - Agentic RAG, Hybrid RAG, and Self-Correction Patterns
19.1 Agentic RAG
docs.text: Agent decides when to retrieve, from where, and how to use evidence.

docs.title: 19.2 Hybrid RAG
docs.text: Blend vector retrieval, lexical retrieval, and structured sources.

docs.title: 19.3 Self-Correction
docs.text: Add critique and verification passes before final answers.

docs.title: 19.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Require citations and confidence thresholds for high-stakes responses.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Policy agent re-queries missing evidence when citation confidence is too low.

docs.title: 20 - Advanced Context Compression and Memory Lifecycle Design
20.1 Context Budgeting
docs.text: Prioritize critical context slices under strict token limits.

docs.title: 20.2 Compression Strategies
docs.text: Use summarization, salience scoring, and semantic deduplication.

docs.title: 20.3 Memory Lifecycle
docs.text: Define creation, update, archival, and deletion policies for long-term memory.

docs.title: 20.4 Drift Prevention
docs.text: Validate memory relevance and decay stale information over time.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support agent compresses long ticket history into policy-aligned memory notes.

docs.title: 21.1 MCP Role
docs.text: Model Context Protocol standardizes access to external tools and resources.

docs.title: 21.2 Integration Pattern
docs.text: Connect MCP servers as structured capability sources for agent workflows.

docs.title: 21.3 Security Considerations
docs.text: Apply capability scoping, authorization checks, and audit logging.

docs.title: 21.4 Operational Benefits
docs.text: Reusable protocol contracts simplify integration across environments.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: LangChain agent accesses CRM and policy resources through MCP server connectors.

docs.title: 22 - Advanced Streaming, Realtime UX, and Event-Driven Agents
22.1 Streaming Modes
docs.text: Stream tokens, tool events, and intermediate state transitions.

docs.title: 22.2 Event-Driven Architecture
docs.text: Trigger agent workflows from external events and webhooks.

docs.title: 22.3 UX Design
docs.text: Expose progress, confidence, and pending approvals to users in real time.

docs.title: 22.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Handle dropped streams with resume-safe state and retry logic.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident bot streams investigation steps while new alerts dynamically update context.

docs.title: 23 - Testing, Evaluation, and Quality Gates with LangSmith
23.1 Test Strategy
docs.text: Combine unit tests, integration tests, and scenario-based agent tests.

docs.title: 23.2 Evaluation Design
docs.text: Measure correctness, tool-use quality, citation quality, and policy compliance.

docs.title: 23.3 LangSmith Workflows
docs.text: Use traces, datasets, and experiments for systematic evaluation.

docs.title: 23.4 Release Gates
docs.text: Block deployment on failed quality thresholds.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Release pipeline rejects prompt update after evaluation shows rising hallucination rate.

docs.title: 24 - Observability, Tracing, Debugging, and Incident Analysis
24.1 Tracing Model
docs.text: Capture full execution traces across model calls, tool calls, and middleware.

docs.title: 24.2 Operational Metrics
docs.text: Track latency, token usage, tool failure rates, and abandonment events.

docs.title: 24.3 Debugging Workflow
docs.text: Correlate traces with prompt versions and configuration snapshots.

docs.title: 24.4 Incident Analysis
docs.text: Use structured postmortems to identify root cause and prevention controls.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Trace analysis reveals tool timeout cascade causing degraded response quality.

docs.title: 25.1 Deployment Models
docs.text: Choose serverless, containerized, or hybrid agent deployments by workload profile.

docs.title: 25.2 Runtime Isolation
docs.text: Separate staging and production execution environments with strict config controls.

docs.title: 25.3 Release Engineering
docs.text: Use canary releases, feature flags, and rollback pipelines.

docs.title: 25.4 Operational Playbooks
docs.text: Define runbooks for degraded models, failed tools, and policy incidents.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Production team rolls back unsafe prompt release after policy-violation spike.

docs.title: 26.1 Security Layers
docs.text: Protect prompts, memory stores, tool endpoints, and model credentials.

docs.title: 26.2 Privacy Controls
docs.text: Implement PII redaction, retention limits, and data minimization.

docs.title: 26.3 Compliance Framework
docs.text: Align agent behavior with legal and internal policy requirements.

docs.title: 26.4 Governance Model
docs.text: Assign ownership for prompts, tools, evaluations, and release approvals.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Healthcare agent enforces PHI masking and audit logging before external API calls.

docs.title: 27 - Performance, Concurrency, Scaling, and Capacity Planning
27.1 Throughput Engineering
docs.text: Optimize concurrent thread handling and tool execution bottlenecks.

docs.title: 27.2 Latency Budgeting
docs.text: Allocate latency targets per step: model call, retrieval, tools, and post-processing.

docs.title: 27.3 Capacity Planning
docs.text: Forecast compute, memory, and token budgets from traffic patterns.

docs.title: 27.4 Cost-Performance Balance
docs.text: Tune response quality against response time and operating cost.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Agent API doubles throughput by parallelizing independent tool calls and caching retrieval.

docs.title: 28 - Migration to LangChain v1: Namespace and Compatibility Strategy
28.1 Migration Drivers
docs.text: v1 introduces stable agent patterns and simplified package organization.

docs.title: 28.2 Breaking-Change Mapping
docs.text: Identify deprecated APIs, namespace updates, and behavior changes.

docs.title: 28.3 Compatibility Plan
docs.text: Use phased migration with dual-path validation and feature toggles.

docs.title: 28.4 Verification
docs.text: Run regression tests and trace comparisons before full cutover.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team migrates from legacy chains to create_agent with zero production downtime.

docs.title: 29 - Enterprise Reference Architectures and Integration Patterns
29.1 Reference Stack
docs.text: Gateway -> agent runtime -> tool tier -> memory/retrieval -> evaluation/observability.

docs.title: 29.2 Integration Patterns
docs.text: Integrate LangChain with APIs, event buses, databases, and governance services.

docs.title: 29.3 Domain Templates
docs.text: Adapt architecture for support, finance, healthcare, and operations use cases.

docs.title: 29.4 Platform Controls
docs.text: Standardize security, release, and quality policies across all agents.

docs.title: 29.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Enterprise agent platform centralizes tool access and policy enforcement for multiple teams.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: Design a Production Agent Platform
30.1 Capstone Goal
docs.text: Design a full LangChain-based agent platform with production governance.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text: Architecture diagram, tool catalog, memory model, evaluation strategy, and SLO plan.

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text: Assess reliability, security, scalability, and operational readiness.

docs.title: 30.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Capstone team presents regulated customer-assistant platform with HITL and full tracing.
