docs.title: LangChain for Agentic AI
docs.text: This folder contains a complete Foundations + Advanced documentation set.

docs.title: Open First
docs.text: • INDEX.md

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
1. Foundations Track: chapters 01-15
  2. Advanced Track: chapters 16-30

docs.title: 1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build complete production-level expertise in LangGraph for durable agent systems.

docs.title: 1.2 Framework Positioning
docs.text: LangGraph provides low-level graph orchestration for stateful and controllable agent workflows.

docs.title: 1.3 Core Components
docs.text: StateGraph, nodes, edges, reducers, checkpoints, interrupts, and runtime hooks.

docs.title: 1.4 Why LangGraph
docs.text: It enables deterministic control where standard agent loops are insufficient.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support pipeline pauses for approval, resumes, and records state for auditability.

docs.title: 2.1 Package Setup
docs.text: Install LangGraph with required model/provider integrations.

docs.title: 2.2 Environment Strategy
docs.text: Use isolated environments and secure configuration for API credentials.

docs.title: 2.3 Project Structure
docs.text: Organize by graph definitions, nodes, tool adapters, and test suites.

docs.title: 2.4 Runtime Config
docs.text: Version model settings, retry policies, and checkpoint storage config.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team runs the same graph in local and production by swapping config only.

docs.title: 03 - LangGraph Architecture, StateGraph, and Execution Model
3.1 StateGraph Concept
docs.text: StateGraph coordinates node execution through explicit state transitions.

docs.title: 3.2 Execution Semantics
docs.text: Each node reads and writes state in controlled, typed pathways.

docs.title: 3.3 Graph Lifecycle
docs.text: Graph compilation creates executable workflow plans from definitions.

docs.title: 3.4 Determinism vs Flexibility
docs.text: Graph structure controls flow while node logic can remain dynamic.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident workflow executes triage -> diagnosis -> remediation nodes with shared state.

docs.title: 4.1 Node Responsibilities
docs.text: Nodes encapsulate distinct steps such as retrieval, reasoning, or action.

docs.title: 4.2 Edge Types
docs.text: Use static and conditional edges to route execution between nodes.

docs.title: 4.3 Commands
docs.text: Commands can direct graph traversal and control branching behavior.

docs.title: 4.4 Routing Design
docs.text: Router nodes centralize branching logic for maintainable orchestration.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Router sends billing issues to finance branch and outages to incident branch.

docs.title: 5.1 State Schema
docs.text: Define typed state contracts to ensure predictable graph behavior.

docs.title: 5.2 Channels
docs.text: Use channels to isolate logical state segments across workflow stages.

docs.title: 5.3 Reducers
docs.text: Reducers merge updates safely when multiple nodes write concurrently.

docs.title: 5.4 Schema Evolution
docs.text: Version state contracts to support upgrades without breaking runs.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Parallel retrieval nodes merge evidence lists through deterministic reducer logic.

docs.title: 6.1 Persistence Purpose
docs.text: Checkpointing allows workflow resumption after failures or pauses.

docs.title: 6.2 Checkpoint Stores
docs.text: Use durable backing stores for production-grade recoverability.

docs.title: 6.3 Resume Semantics
docs.text: Restart from last consistent checkpoint instead of replaying full workflow.

docs.title: 6.4 Operational Controls
docs.text: Design retention and cleanup policies for checkpoint storage.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Long-running compliance flow resumes after restart without losing approval history.

docs.title: 7.1 Interrupt Model
docs.text: Interrupts pause execution before high-risk steps.

docs.title: 7.2 Approval Workflows
docs.text: Human checkpoints validate sensitive actions and uncertain outcomes.

docs.title: 7.3 Resume Control
docs.text: Approved runs resume with preserved context and state continuity.

docs.title: 7.4 Governance
docs.text: Audit logs capture approval actors, timestamps, and reasons.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Payment action pauses for manager approval before external transfer tool call.

docs.title: 8.1 Short-Term Memory
docs.text: Thread-local memory retains immediate conversational context.

docs.title: 8.2 Long-Term Memory
docs.text: Persistent memory stores user, domain, or workflow knowledge.

docs.title: 8.3 Memory Boundaries
docs.text: Separate session context from durable profile memory.

docs.title: 8.4 Lifecycle Policies
docs.text: Define memory retention, summarization, and deletion workflows.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support assistant recalls account preferences while pruning stale history.

docs.title: 09 - Tool Calling and External System Integration
9.1 Tool Contracts
docs.text: Tools should expose strict schemas and stable response formats.

docs.title: 9.2 Integration Patterns
docs.text: Connect APIs, databases, and internal services through dedicated nodes.

docs.title: 9.3 Safety Boundaries
docs.text: Validate arguments and apply least-privilege authorization for tool use.

docs.title: 9.4 Reliability
docs.text: Add retries and idempotency handling for side-effecting actions.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Agent calls CRM and ticket tools to assemble escalation context.

docs.title: 10.1 Multi-Agent Model
docs.text: Use specialized agents as nodes in shared graph workflows.

docs.title: 10.2 Coordination Patterns
docs.text: Supervisor nodes route tasks to domain-specific worker agents.

docs.title: 10.3 State Sharing
docs.text: Share only required context to reduce noise and leakage.

docs.title: 10.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Bound interaction loops with explicit termination policies.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Research, critique, and synthesis agents collaborate in one governed graph.

docs.title: 11 - Streaming Events and Realtime Agent UX
11.1 Streaming Model
docs.text: Stream tokens, state updates, and tool events for responsive UX.

docs.title: 11.2 Event Handling
docs.text: Consumers render partial outputs and status changes incrementally.

docs.title: 11.3 Reliability
docs.text: Handle dropped streams with resumable state and replay-safe events.

docs.title: 11.4 UX Design
docs.text: Expose progress and pending approvals to improve transparency.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident assistant streams triage progress before final remediation recommendation.

docs.title: 12.1 Subgraph Concept
docs.text: Subgraphs package reusable workflow logic for composability.

docs.title: 12.2 Interface Contracts
docs.text: Define clear input/output state boundaries for subgraph modules.

docs.title: 12.3 Reuse Patterns
docs.text: Compose standard retrieval, approval, and reporting subgraphs.

docs.title: 12.4 Maintenance Benefits
docs.text: Modularity simplifies testing, versioning, and team ownership.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Shared approval subgraph is reused across finance and legal workflows.

docs.title: 13.1 Test Strategy
docs.text: Use unit tests for nodes and integration tests for end-to-end graphs.

docs.title: 13.2 Evaluation Baseline
docs.text: Measure quality, policy adherence, latency, and task completion rates.

docs.title: 13.3 Observability Signals
docs.text: Track traces, state transitions, and tool error rates.

docs.title: 13.4 Release Gates
docs.text: Block deployments when critical quality metrics regress.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Regression tests catch unsafe branch transition before production rollout.

docs.title: 14.1 Deployment Options
docs.text: Deploy graphs as APIs, background workers, or event-driven services.

docs.title: 14.2 Runtime Hardening
docs.text: Enable checkpoint storage, rate limits, and secret management.

docs.title: 14.3 Release Engineering
docs.text: Use canary rollout, feature flags, and fast rollback.

docs.title: 14.4 Operability
docs.text: Prepare runbooks for outages, tool failures, and policy incidents.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Production graph rolls back automatically after elevated failure-rate alert.

docs.title: 15 - Labs, Projects, Glossary, and Operational Checklists
Lab 1
docs.text: Build a typed StateGraph with conditional routing and reducer merges.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Add durable checkpointing and restart-safe resume behavior.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Implement human approval interrupt before sensitive tool actions.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Deploy graph service with telemetry and regression gate checks.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• state graph
  • reducer
  • checkpoint
  • interrupt
  • subgraph

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• state schema validated
  • safety approvals enabled
  • observability dashboards configured
  • rollback plan tested

docs.title: 16 - Advanced Graph Design, Concurrency, and Determinism
16.1 Concurrency Model
docs.text: Design parallel branches with explicit merge points and reducer logic.

docs.title: 16.2 Deterministic Behavior
docs.text: Constrain non-deterministic node effects with idempotent state transitions.

docs.title: 16.3 Merge Semantics
docs.text: Use typed reducers to resolve concurrent writes safely.

docs.title: 16.4 Scalability Pattern
docs.text: Partition workflows into composable subgraphs for maintainable growth.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Parallel risk checks merge into a deterministic approval decision node.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Control Flow: Loops, Retries, and Error Boundaries
17.1 Loop Patterns
docs.text: Use bounded loops for iterative refinement and fallback recovery.

docs.title: 17.2 Retry Policies
docs.text: Apply error-type-aware retries with backoff and stop conditions.

docs.title: 17.3 Error Boundaries
docs.text: Isolate failures to subgraphs and avoid global execution collapse.

docs.title: 17.4 Compensation Paths
docs.text: Design undo/compensation steps for partial side effects.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Failed enrichment node retries with alternate provider before triggering manual review.

docs.title: 18 - Supervisor, Swarm, and Hierarchical Multi-Agent Architectures
18.1 Supervisor Pattern
docs.text: Central coordinator assigns subtasks and consolidates outputs.

docs.title: 18.2 Swarm Pattern
docs.text: Peer agents collaborate with lightweight coordination for adaptive discovery.

docs.title: 18.3 Hierarchical Pattern
docs.text: Nested teams support complex domain decomposition at scale.

docs.title: 18.4 Governance Controls
docs.text: Limit delegation depth, tool scope, and cross-agent data exposure.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident commander agent orchestrates diagnosis swarm and escalation hierarchy.

docs.title: 19 - Time Travel, Replay, and Debugging at Scale
19.1 Time-Travel Debugging
docs.text: Inspect prior graph states to analyze decision paths.

docs.title: 19.2 Replay Workflows
docs.text: Replay runs with controlled inputs to reproduce failures.

docs.title: 19.3 Root-Cause Analysis
docs.text: Correlate node transitions, tool outputs, and state mutations.

docs.title: 19.4 Operational Value
docs.text: Shortens incident resolution and improves regression prevention.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team replays failed workflow to isolate incorrect router condition.

docs.title: 20 - Advanced Persistence, Fault Tolerance, and Recovery
20.1 Durability Architecture
docs.text: Persist checkpoints across distributed runtime instances.

docs.title: 20.2 Fault Tolerance
docs.text: Recover from node, network, and dependency failures without data loss.

docs.title: 20.3 Recovery Playbooks
docs.text: Resume from stable checkpoints with validation before continuation.

docs.title: 20.4 Storage Governance
docs.text: Apply retention and archival rules for long-lived graph histories.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Workflow resumes after outage from last approved checkpoint and completes safely.

docs.title: 21 - Advanced Human Governance, Policy Gates, and Approvals
21.1 Governance Layer
docs.text: Embed policy gates at high-impact transitions.

docs.title: 21.2 Approval Design
docs.text: Route approvals by role, risk level, and jurisdiction.

docs.title: 21.3 Escalation Flow
docs.text: Handle rejected actions with remediation or alternative workflow branches.

docs.title: 21.4 Audit Requirements
docs.text: Capture decision rationale, approver identity, and compliance metadata.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Procurement graph requires dual approval before contract execution node.

docs.title: 22 - LangGraph with MCP, A2A, and External Protocol Interoperability
22.1 Interoperability Strategy
docs.text: Combine LangGraph orchestration with protocol-standard tool/agent ecosystems.

docs.title: 22.2 MCP Integration
docs.text: Use MCP for portable tool and resource access across environments.

docs.title: 22.3 A2A Integration
docs.text: Coordinate cross-agent delegation through standardized A2A contracts.

docs.title: 22.4 Security Controls
docs.text: Apply scoped permissions and protocol-level audit tracing.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Coordinator graph delegates external specialist task via A2A and consumes MCP tools locally.

docs.title: 23 - Performance Tuning, Latency, Cost, and Throughput Optimization
23.1 Performance Model
docs.text: Measure node execution latency, tool wait time, and queue delays.

docs.title: 23.2 Cost Controls
docs.text: Optimize model routing, context size, and tool invocation frequency.

docs.title: 23.3 Throughput Design
docs.text: Parallelize independent branches and constrain bottleneck nodes.

docs.title: 23.4 Optimization Loop
docs.text: Use telemetry-driven tuning with safe rollback thresholds.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Routing changes reduce p95 latency while preserving answer quality targets.

docs.title: 24 - Security, Privacy, Compliance, and Tenant Isolation
24.1 Security Boundaries
docs.text: Protect graph runtime, tool endpoints, and checkpoint storage.

docs.title: 24.2 Privacy Controls
docs.text: Mask sensitive data and enforce data minimization in state transitions.

docs.title: 24.3 Tenant Isolation
docs.text: Partition state, memory, and tool credentials by tenant context.

docs.title: 24.4 Compliance Model
docs.text: Map controls to internal policy and regulatory frameworks.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Multi-tenant assistant blocks cross-tenant retrieval through namespace isolation.

docs.title: 25 - EvalOps, Regression Gates, and CI/CD for Agent Systems
25.1 EvalOps Framework
docs.text: Integrate automated quality evaluation into deployment pipeline.

docs.title: 25.2 Regression Suites
docs.text: Test path correctness, policy compliance, and response quality.

docs.title: 25.3 CI/CD Gates
docs.text: Block releases when critical metrics regress.

docs.title: 25.4 Continuous Improvement
docs.text: Feed production incidents back into test and evaluation suites.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Release is blocked after regression run detects unsafe branch behavior.

docs.title: 26 - Observability, Incident Response, and SLO Management
26.1 Observability Stack
docs.text: Capture traces, logs, state transitions, and domain KPIs.

docs.title: 26.2 SLO Definition
docs.text: Define SLOs for reliability, latency, and safety outcomes.

docs.title: 26.3 Incident Lifecycle
docs.text: Detect, triage, contain, recover, and review with structured runbooks.

docs.title: 26.4 Postmortem Discipline
docs.text: Document root causes and preventive changes after each incident.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: On-call team isolates failing tool dependency and restores SLO through fallback node.

docs.title: 27 - Advanced Deployment Topologies: Kubernetes, Serverless, and Edge
27.1 Topology Selection
docs.text: Choose deployment model by latency, scale, and governance constraints.

docs.title: 27.2 Kubernetes Pattern
docs.text: Use containerized graph workers with autoscaling and policy controls.

docs.title: 27.3 Serverless and Edge
docs.text: Use event-driven runtimes for bursty loads and low-latency regional serving.

docs.title: 27.4 Reliability Design
docs.text: Implement health checks, retries, and regional failover plans.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Global support graph serves region-local requests from edge workers.

docs.title: 28 - Migration Strategies: LangChain Agents to LangGraph
28.1 Migration Drivers
docs.text: Move to LangGraph for greater control, durability, and deterministic orchestration.

docs.title: 28.2 Mapping Strategy
docs.text: Map legacy agent loops to explicit graph nodes and state contracts.

docs.title: 28.3 Phased Rollout
docs.text: Use dual-run validation and canary migration to reduce risk.

docs.title: 28.4 Compatibility Controls
docs.text: Preserve tool contracts and evaluation baselines during migration.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team migrates high-risk workflows first to gain approval and checkpoint controls.

docs.title: 29 - Enterprise Reference Architectures and Domain Blueprints
29.1 Reference Stack
docs.text: Ingress -> graph runtime -> tool plane -> data plane -> governance/observability.

docs.title: 29.2 Domain Blueprints
docs.text: Create reusable templates for support, finance, healthcare, and operations.

docs.title: 29.3 Control Plane
docs.text: Centralize policy, release, and telemetry standards.

docs.title: 29.4 Platform Governance
docs.text: Define ownership for graphs, subgraphs, and shared tool assets.

docs.title: 29.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Enterprise platform standardizes workflow modules across departments with local customization.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: Design a Production LangGraph Platform
30.1 Capstone Goal
docs.text: Design a production LangGraph platform with reliability, governance, and scalability controls.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text: Architecture diagram, state model, approval design, evaluation plan, and SLO framework.

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text: Assess reliability, safety, cost-efficiency, and operational maturity.

docs.title: 30.4 Failure Simulation
docs.text: Include outage recovery, rollback steps, and post-incident improvement plan.

docs.title: 30.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Capstone system automates enterprise support with auditable approvals and durable execution.

docs.title: LangGraph for Agentic AI
docs.text: This folder contains a complete Foundations + Advanced documentation set.

docs.title: Open First
docs.text: • INDEX.md

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
1. Foundations Track: chapters 01-15
  2. Advanced Track: chapters 16-30

docs.title: 01 - Layer 01: Business Objectives and Value Strategy
1.1 Purpose
docs.text: Define measurable business outcomes the agentic platform must produce.

docs.title: 1.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Prioritize revenue, risk reduction, efficiency, and user-experience targets.

docs.title: 1.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Value hypothesis, KPI tree, and success criteria per domain.

docs.title: 1.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: No clear value target leads to technically correct but low-impact systems.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support automation is scoped to cut ticket resolution time by 30%.

docs.title: 2.1 Purpose
docs.text: Select the right sequence of agentic use cases for staged rollout.

docs.title: 2.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Rank use cases by feasibility, risk, data readiness, and business impact.

docs.title: 2.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Portfolio matrix, rollout waves, and dependency mapping.

docs.title: 2.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: High-risk use cases launched too early create trust and adoption issues.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team starts with internal knowledge assistant before autonomous customer actions.

docs.title: 03 - Layer 03: Policy Governance and Responsible AI
3.1 Purpose
docs.text: Embed legal, ethical, and organizational policies into runtime decisions.

docs.title: 3.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define content rules, domain boundaries, and escalation requirements.

docs.title: 3.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Policy catalog, risk tiers, and governance workflows.

docs.title: 3.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unenforced policy causes compliance breaches and reputational risk.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Financial recommendations require disclosure policy and human approval.

docs.title: 04 - Layer 04: Identity, Access, and Trust Controls
4.1 Purpose
docs.text: Ensure only authorized actors and agents can access sensitive capabilities.

docs.title: 4.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Implement least-privilege IAM, scoped tokens, and trust boundaries.

docs.title: 4.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Role model, access policy matrix, and credential lifecycle plan.

docs.title: 4.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Over-privileged agents can trigger unauthorized data and tool actions.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Billing tools are only available to finance-role agents.

docs.title: 05 - Layer 05: Data Sources and Ingestion Pipelines
5.1 Purpose
docs.text: Bring enterprise data into the agentic system with controlled freshness.

docs.title: 5.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Choose connectors, update cadence, and ingestion reliability model.

docs.title: 5.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Source inventory, ingestion DAGs, and freshness SLOs.

docs.title: 5.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Stale or partial data leads to incorrect agent reasoning.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: CRM and policy documents ingest hourly with change-data-capture updates.

docs.title: 06 - Layer 06: Data Quality, Lineage, and Catalog
6.1 Purpose
docs.text: Guarantee data trustworthiness and traceability for every agent decision.

docs.title: 6.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define validation rules, lineage capture, and metadata standards.

docs.title: 6.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Data quality checks, lineage graph, and catalog taxonomy.

docs.title: 6.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Untraceable data prevents audit and root-cause analysis.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Answer citations include source system, timestamp, and transformation history.

docs.title: 07 - Layer 07: Knowledge Modeling and Retrieval Substrate
7.1 Purpose
docs.text: Create a reliable substrate for semantic and structured retrieval.

docs.title: 7.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Use vector, graph, keyword, or hybrid retrieval depending on domain needs.

docs.title: 7.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Retrieval schema, chunking policy, and metadata filter model.

docs.title: 7.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Weak retrieval substrate increases hallucinations and unsupported actions.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Policy QA system uses hybrid retrieval with citation-first response rules.

docs.title: 08 - Layer 08: Model Serving and Inference Gateway
8.1 Purpose
docs.text: Standardize model access and enforce routing, limits, and fallback behaviors.

docs.title: 8.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Select model tiers by task complexity, latency, and compliance constraints.

docs.title: 8.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Model routing policy, fallback matrix, and budget controls.

docs.title: 8.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unmanaged model usage drives cost spikes and reliability issues.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Low-risk FAQ uses cheaper model; legal drafting uses high-accuracy model.

docs.title: 09 - Layer 09: Prompt, Context, and Instruction Management
9.1 Purpose
docs.text: Control how agents are instructed and grounded at runtime.

docs.title: 9.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define prompt versioning, context assembly, and instruction hierarchy.

docs.title: 9.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Prompt registry, context policies, and template governance workflow.

docs.title: 9.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Prompt drift causes inconsistent behavior and compliance regressions.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Release is blocked when prompt change reduces citation compliance.

docs.title: 10.1 Purpose
docs.text: Connect agents to APIs, systems, and automations through governed action contracts.

docs.title: 10.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Set tool schemas, permission scopes, and side-effect classifications.

docs.title: 10.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Tool catalog, action policy rules, and timeout/retry settings.

docs.title: 10.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unsafe tool invocation can trigger unauthorized or irreversible actions.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Wire-transfer tool requires policy pass and approval before execution.

docs.title: 11 - Layer 11: Agent Orchestration and Workflow Control
11.1 Purpose
docs.text: Coordinate planning, delegation, and execution through explicit workflow logic.

docs.title: 11.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Choose supervisor, planner-executor, or graph-state orchestration patterns.

docs.title: 11.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Workflow graph, state contracts, and routing rules.

docs.title: 11.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Implicit orchestration leads to non-deterministic and hard-to-debug behavior.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident agent graph routes triage, remediation, and approval branches.

docs.title: 12.1 Purpose
docs.text: Preserve relevant context across interactions while enforcing safety boundaries.

docs.title: 12.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Separate session memory from long-term profile memory.

docs.title: 12.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Memory schema, retention policy, and personalization controls.

docs.title: 12.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unbounded memory creates privacy risk and context noise.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Assistant remembers customer preferences but expires stale incident details.

docs.title: 13 - Layer 13: Human Approval and Exception Handling
13.1 Purpose
docs.text: Route high-risk decisions and unexpected failures to human supervision.

docs.title: 13.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define interrupt triggers and escalation paths by risk tier.

docs.title: 13.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Approval workflow, exception taxonomy, and fallback playbooks.

docs.title: 13.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: No human override path increases operational and compliance risk.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Contract finalization pauses until legal approver signs off.

docs.title: 14 - Layer 14: Evaluation, Safety, and Quality Gates
14.1 Purpose
docs.text: Ensure agents meet quality and safety standards before and after release.

docs.title: 14.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Set evaluation metrics, red-team criteria, and release thresholds.

docs.title: 14.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Eval datasets, safety policies, and regression gate pipeline.

docs.title: 14.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unchecked model updates degrade reliability and policy adherence.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Canary deployment halts after faithfulness metric drops below threshold.

docs.title: 15 - Layer 15: Observability, SLOs, and Incident Response
15.1 Purpose
docs.text: Provide continuous visibility into behavior, reliability, and user impact.

docs.title: 15.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define SLOs for latency, success rate, and safety outcomes.

docs.title: 15.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Telemetry model, alerting rules, and incident runbooks.

docs.title: 15.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Poor observability delays detection and increases outage impact.

docs.title: 15.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: On-call detects rising tool timeouts and activates fallback mode.

docs.title: 16 - Layer 16: Operations, SRE, and Lifecycle Management
16.1 Purpose
docs.text: Operate agentic systems reliably through release, maintenance, and retirement.

docs.title: 16.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define ownership, on-call rotations, and lifecycle governance.

docs.title: 16.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Runbooks, change calendar, and deprecation strategy.

docs.title: 16.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: No lifecycle discipline causes operational drift and unmanaged risk.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: SRE workflow enforces canary rollout and auto-rollback on SLA breach.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Layer 01: Enterprise Strategy and AI FinOps
17.1 Purpose
docs.text: Align enterprise AI architecture with portfolio-level value and cost controls.

docs.title: 17.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Set budget policies, ROI targets, and strategic prioritization rules.

docs.title: 17.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: AI FinOps dashboard, budget guardrails, and portfolio scorecards.

docs.title: 17.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: No cost governance leads to uncontrolled inference and tooling spend.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Platform enforces monthly token budgets per business unit.

docs.title: 18 - Advanced Layer 02: Regulatory Controls and Compliance by Design
18.1 Purpose
docs.text: Embed regulatory requirements into architecture rather than post-release patches.

docs.title: 18.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Map regulations to data handling, approvals, and model behavior controls.

docs.title: 18.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Control matrix, compliance traces, and automated policy checks.

docs.title: 18.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Late compliance retrofits cause delays and legal exposure.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Healthcare workflows enforce jurisdiction-specific retention and masking policies.

docs.title: 19 - Advanced Layer 03: Zero-Trust Identity and Federated Authorization
19.1 Purpose
docs.text: Continuously verify identity and authorization for every agent action.

docs.title: 19.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Adopt federated identity, scoped tokens, and context-aware authorization.

docs.title: 19.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Trust policy engine, federation map, and token lifecycle controls.

docs.title: 19.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Implicit trust between services creates lateral movement risk.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Agent re-authorizes sensitive API action on every high-risk step.

docs.title: 20 - Advanced Layer 04: API Mesh, Event Bus, and System Connectivity
20.1 Purpose
docs.text: Provide reliable connectivity between agents and enterprise systems.

docs.title: 20.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Use API mesh for synchronous calls and event bus for asynchronous workflows.

docs.title: 20.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Service catalog, event taxonomy, and connectivity SLA matrix.

docs.title: 20.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Point-to-point integrations create brittle dependencies and scaling limits.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Ticket lifecycle events trigger downstream agents through standardized topics.

docs.title: 21 - Advanced Layer 05: Data Products, Lakehouse, and Streaming Backbone
21.1 Purpose
docs.text: Establish data backbone for batch and real-time agentic workloads.

docs.title: 21.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define data products, ownership, and streaming update mechanisms.

docs.title: 21.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Lakehouse zones, stream contracts, and data product SLA definitions.

docs.title: 21.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Fragmented data platforms create inconsistent agent context.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Fraud signals stream into shared feature and retrieval systems within seconds.

docs.title: 22 - Advanced Layer 06: Unstructured Processing and Content Engineering
22.1 Purpose
docs.text: Convert unstructured enterprise content into reliable machine-usable assets.

docs.title: 22.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Choose parsing pipelines, chunking strategy, and metadata enrichment depth.

docs.title: 22.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Content transformation DAGs, parser registry, and quality validation suite.

docs.title: 22.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Poor parsing introduces silent errors that propagate into agent decisions.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: PDF policy docs are normalized into section-aware chunks with legal metadata.

docs.title: 23 - Advanced Layer 07: Knowledge Graphs, Vector DB, and Hybrid Retrieval
23.1 Purpose
docs.text: Combine semantic and relational retrieval for high-precision reasoning.

docs.title: 23.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Blend vector similarity, graph traversal, and keyword constraints.

docs.title: 23.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Hybrid retrieval orchestrator, ranking policy, and source-confidence model.

docs.title: 23.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Single-mode retrieval misses critical context in complex domains.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Risk assistant joins graph-based entity links with vector policy retrieval.

docs.title: 24 - Advanced Layer 08: Feature Store and Semantic Memory Fabric
24.1 Purpose
docs.text: Provide unified feature and memory plane for prediction and personalization.

docs.title: 24.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Separate real-time features from durable semantic memory artifacts.

docs.title: 24.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Feature registry, memory namespaces, and retention/access policies.

docs.title: 24.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Memory-feature coupling without controls causes stale or unsafe personalization.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support agent combines live account features with long-term preference memory.

docs.title: 25 - Advanced Layer 09: Model Gateway, Routing, and Inference Optimization
25.1 Purpose
docs.text: Centralize model access, routing, and cost-performance optimization.

docs.title: 25.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Route by complexity, compliance tier, latency SLO, and confidence.

docs.title: 25.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Routing policy graph, fallback matrix, and budget throttling controls.

docs.title: 25.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unmanaged model sprawl causes inconsistent quality and exploding cost.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Gateway escalates only high-risk legal prompts to premium models.

docs.title: 26 - Advanced Layer 10: PromptOps, ContextOps, and Policy-Injected Reasoning
26.1 Purpose
docs.text: Operationalize prompt and context management as governed platform capabilities.

docs.title: 26.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Version prompts, context builders, and policy-injection middleware.

docs.title: 26.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Prompt registry, context assembly templates, and approval workflow.

docs.title: 26.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Uncontrolled prompt edits create behavioral drift and compliance regressions.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: PromptOps pipeline blocks deployment when policy clauses are removed.

docs.title: 27 - Advanced Layer 11: Tooling, Action Policy, and Execution Sandboxing
27.1 Purpose
docs.text: Enable safe external actions through strict policy and isolation.

docs.title: 27.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Classify tool risk, require confirmations, and sandbox untrusted execution.

docs.title: 27.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Tool risk matrix, action guardrails, and sandbox runtime policy.

docs.title: 27.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Direct unrestricted execution creates security and operational hazards.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Generated code runs in sandbox with egress restrictions and audit logs.

docs.title: 28 - Advanced Layer 12: Agent Runtime Kernel and State Graph Engine
28.1 Purpose
docs.text: Provide deterministic, durable execution for complex agent workflows.

docs.title: 28.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Use explicit state graphs, reducers, and checkpoint semantics.

docs.title: 28.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Runtime kernel interface, state contracts, and execution governance rules.

docs.title: 28.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Implicit runtime state causes non-reproducible failures and debugging pain.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Workflow resumes after outage from latest approved checkpoint.

docs.title: 29 - Advanced Layer 13: Multi-Agent Supervision and Delegation Topology
29.1 Purpose
docs.text: Scale agent collaboration with explicit supervision and delegation control.

docs.title: 29.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define supervisor logic, delegation depth, and handoff contracts.

docs.title: 29.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Delegation topology map, role permissions, and escalation paths.

docs.title: 29.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unchecked delegation loops increase cost and decision risk.

docs.title: 29.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Supervisor routes financial analysis to specialists and enforces final validation.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Layer 14: Planning, Reasoning, and Verification Loops
30.1 Purpose
docs.text: Increase answer reliability with structured planning and independent verification.

docs.title: 30.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Separate reasoning generation from verifier checks and evidence confirmation.

docs.title: 30.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Plan schema, verifier rubric, and loop termination policy.

docs.title: 30.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unverified reasoning can produce plausible but incorrect outcomes.

docs.title: 30.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Verifier triggers re-planning when citation confidence is below threshold.

docs.title: 31 - Advanced Layer 15: Human Governance, Approvals, and Audit Trails
31.1 Purpose
docs.text: Embed accountable human oversight into enterprise agent actions.

docs.title: 31.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define approval roles, thresholds, and exception workflows.

docs.title: 31.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Approval matrix, audit schema, and compliance evidence store.

docs.title: 31.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: No audit trail undermines accountability and legal defensibility.

docs.title: 31.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: High-value procurement actions require two-level approval and full trace logs.

docs.title: 32 - Advanced Layer 16: Reliability, Resilience, and Fault Recovery
32.1 Purpose
docs.text: Maintain service continuity under model, tool, and infrastructure failures.

docs.title: 32.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Design retries, circuit breakers, checkpoint resume, and compensation logic.

docs.title: 32.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Failure mode catalog, resilience test suite, and recovery runbooks.

docs.title: 32.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: No recovery orchestration causes prolonged outages and inconsistent state.

docs.title: 32.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: System fails over to fallback model tier during primary outage.

docs.title: 33 - Advanced Layer 17: Security Monitoring and Threat Detection
33.1 Purpose
docs.text: Detect and respond to adversarial behavior targeting agent workflows.

docs.title: 33.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Monitor prompt injection, tool abuse, exfiltration, and privilege escalation.

docs.title: 33.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Threat model, detection rules, and automated containment playbooks.

docs.title: 33.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Blind spots in monitoring delay detection of high-impact attacks.

docs.title: 33.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Policy engine blocks suspicious tool invocation pattern and triggers incident alert.

docs.title: 34 - Advanced Layer 18: Observability, Telemetry, and Live Diagnostics
34.1 Purpose
docs.text: Provide deep runtime visibility for proactive operations and debugging.

docs.title: 34.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Instrument traces across reasoning, retrieval, tool calls, and approvals.

docs.title: 34.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Telemetry taxonomy, dashboard suite, and anomaly detection rules.

docs.title: 34.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Limited telemetry obscures root cause and slows remediation.

docs.title: 34.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Live diagnostics reveal reranker latency spike impacting p95 response time.

docs.title: 35 - Advanced Layer 19: Evaluation, Red Teaming, and Continuous Assurance
35.1 Purpose
docs.text: Continuously verify quality, safety, and robustness across production changes.

docs.title: 35.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define eval dimensions, adversarial scenarios, and release thresholds.

docs.title: 35.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Benchmark suites, red-team harness, and continuous assurance pipeline.

docs.title: 35.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Sparse evaluation allows regressions to reach users.

docs.title: 35.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Red-team test catches jailbreak vulnerability before rollout.

docs.title: 36 - Advanced Layer 20: Cost, Performance, and Capacity Orchestration
36.1 Purpose
docs.text: Optimize cost, latency, and throughput under dynamic enterprise demand.

docs.title: 36.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Set adaptive model routing, cache policy, and autoscaling controls.

docs.title: 36.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Capacity model, cost budget policy, and performance tuning playbook.

docs.title: 36.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: No capacity orchestration causes SLA breaches during peak traffic.

docs.title: 36.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Platform autoscaling and route tuning maintain SLA during quarterly spike.

docs.title: 37 - Advanced Layer 21: Deployment Topologies and Platform Governance
37.1 Purpose
docs.text: Standardize runtime deployment across cloud, on-prem, and regulated environments.

docs.title: 37.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Choose topology by latency, sovereignty, and governance requirements.

docs.title: 37.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Topology reference patterns, release policy, and governance guardrails.

docs.title: 37.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Ad-hoc deployments create inconsistent controls and operational drift.

docs.title: 37.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Sensitive workloads run on isolated private cluster with centralized policy control.

docs.title: 38 - Advanced Layer 22: Lifecycle, Migration, and Continuous Improvement
38.1 Purpose
docs.text: Evolve the platform safely through versioning, migration, and learning loops.

docs.title: 38.2 Core Decisions
docs.text: Define migration wave strategy, compatibility checks, and rollback criteria.

docs.title: 38.3 Design Artifacts
docs.text: Lifecycle roadmap, migration matrix, and continuous improvement backlog.

docs.title: 38.4 Failure Mode
docs.text: Unmanaged evolution creates incompatibility and reliability regressions.

docs.title: 38.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Platform upgrades orchestration engine with dual-run validation before full cutover.

docs.title: Agentic AI Architecture - Layers
docs.text: This folder contains two complete architecture sections: 1. Agentic AI Architecture - 16 Layers 2. Advanced Enterprise Agentic AI Architecture - 22 Layers

docs.title: Open First
docs.text: • INDEX.md

docs.title: Agentic AI Architecture - Layers - Index
docs.text: This module uses one index file with two sections: 1. Agentic AI Architecture - 16 Layers 2. Advanced Enterprise Agentic AI Architecture - 22 Layers

docs.title: 1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build complete production-level expertise in LlamaIndex for agentic and retrieval-centric AI systems.

docs.title: 1.2 Framework Positioning
docs.text: LlamaIndex provides a full data-to-agent stack for ingestion, indexing, retrieval, and orchestration.

docs.title: 1.3 Core Components
docs.text: Documents, nodes, indexes, retrievers, query engines, agents, workflows, and observability.

docs.title: 1.4 Learning Path
docs.text: Foundations -> retrieval architecture -> agentic workflows -> enterprise operations.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support assistant retrieves policy evidence and routes complex cases to specialized workflows.

docs.title: 2.1 Package Setup
docs.text: Install llama-index core and only required integration packages.

docs.title: 2.2 Environment Strategy
docs.text: Use isolated environments and secure model/database credential management.

docs.title: 2.3 Project Structure
docs.text: Separate ingestion, indexing, querying, evaluation, and deployment modules.

docs.title: 2.4 Configuration Control
docs.text: Version model settings, retrieval settings, and runtime policies.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team deploys same retrieval app across staging and production by swapping environment configs.

docs.title: 03 - Data Foundations: Documents, Nodes, and Indexes
3.1 Document Model
docs.text: Documents represent raw source data entering the pipeline.

docs.title: 3.2 Node Abstraction
docs.text: Nodes are transformed chunks that carry text, metadata, and relationships.

docs.title: 3.3 Index Role
docs.text: Indexes organize nodes for efficient retrieval under different query patterns.

docs.title: 3.4 Metadata Strategy
docs.text: Metadata quality strongly impacts filtering and answer grounding.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Legal corpus is split into metadata-rich nodes enabling jurisdiction-aware retrieval.

docs.title: 4.1 Loader Layer
docs.text: Load data from files, APIs, cloud stores, and enterprise systems.

docs.title: 4.2 Ingestion Pipeline
docs.text: Pipeline stages normalize content, transform nodes, and attach embeddings.

docs.title: 4.3 Incremental Updates
docs.text: Use change-aware ingestion to keep indexes fresh with low reprocessing cost.

docs.title: 4.4 Data Quality Controls
docs.text: Validate encoding, schema, duplicates, and source integrity.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Knowledge base pipeline ingests ticket updates hourly and refreshes retrievable nodes.

docs.title: 5.1 Parsing Layer
docs.text: Use format-aware parsers for PDFs, markdown, HTML, and structured documents.

docs.title: 5.2 Chunking Design
docs.text: Tune chunk size and overlap by query type and model context window.

docs.title: 5.3 Transformations
docs.text: Apply cleaning, enrichment, and metadata extraction before indexing.

docs.title: 5.4 Tradeoff Management
docs.text: Balance retrieval recall, precision, and cost through chunking strategy.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Finance documents are chunked by section headers to improve targeted retrieval.

docs.title: 06 - Embeddings, Vector Stores, and Storage Architecture
6.1 Embedding Strategy
docs.text: Select embedding models aligned to domain language and retrieval tasks.

docs.title: 6.2 Vector Store Layer
docs.text: Persist embeddings in vector databases with metadata filtering support.

docs.title: 6.3 Storage Design
docs.text: Separate raw data, index artifacts, and cached retrieval results.

docs.title: 6.4 Operational Concerns
docs.text: Monitor embedding drift and re-index when model versions change.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Multilingual support assistant uses language-aware embeddings and filtered vector retrieval.

docs.title: 07 - Indexing Strategies: Vector, Keyword, Tree, and Graph
7.1 Vector Indexing
docs.text: Best for semantic similarity and meaning-based retrieval.

docs.title: 7.2 Keyword and Sparse Patterns
docs.text: Useful for exact terms, identifiers, and compliance-sensitive queries.

docs.title: 7.3 Tree and Graph Indexing
docs.text: Support hierarchical summarization and relationship-aware retrieval.

docs.title: 7.4 Hybrid Design
docs.text: Combine index types for robust enterprise search performance.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident assistant blends vector semantic retrieval with keyword policy matching.

docs.title: 08 - Retrievers, Query Engines, and Router Patterns
8.1 Retriever Role
docs.text: Retrievers select candidate nodes relevant to user intent.

docs.title: 8.2 Query Engine Role
docs.text: Query engines synthesize final responses using retriever outputs.

docs.title: 8.3 Router Patterns
docs.text: Routers dispatch queries to specialized retrievers or engines.

docs.title: 8.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Set fallback retrievers for low-confidence or empty-result scenarios.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Router sends pricing queries to structured index and policy queries to semantic index.

docs.title: 9.1 Synthesis Strategies
docs.text: Use compact, refine, or tree-based response synthesis depending on context size.

docs.title: 9.2 Citation Design
docs.text: Include source references to support traceability and trust.

docs.title: 9.3 Groundedness Checks
docs.text: Detect unsupported claims and trigger re-retrieval where needed.

docs.title: 9.4 Quality Controls
docs.text: Set confidence thresholds and answer refusal policies for weak evidence.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Compliance bot refuses answer when citations do not meet evidence threshold.

docs.title: 10 - Query Planning, SubQuestions, and Tool Routing
10.1 Query Decomposition
docs.text: Break complex questions into sub-questions for targeted retrieval.

docs.title: 10.2 Planning Pipeline
docs.text: Plan -> retrieve -> synthesize -> verify loop improves complex answer quality.

docs.title: 10.3 Tool Routing
docs.text: Route sub-questions to suitable tools or retrievers.

docs.title: 10.4 Termination Logic
docs.text: Stop when coverage and confidence conditions are satisfied.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Operations assistant decomposes outage query into logs, runbooks, and dependency checks.

docs.title: 11 - Chat Engines, Memory, and Conversation Context
11.1 Chat Engine Role
docs.text: Chat engines maintain conversational flow on top of retrieval and tools.

docs.title: 11.2 Memory Strategy
docs.text: Store short-term thread context and selective long-term user memory.

docs.title: 11.3 Context Window Control
docs.text: Summarize and prune old turns to preserve critical context.

docs.title: 11.4 Personalization Boundaries
docs.text: Apply policy controls to avoid unsafe or unnecessary memory use.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer assistant remembers account preferences while respecting retention limits.

docs.title: 12.1 Tool Abstractions
docs.text: Tools expose external capabilities for search, data access, and actions.

docs.title: 12.2 Agent Fundamentals
docs.text: Agents decide when to retrieve, call tools, and synthesize answers.

docs.title: 12.3 Workflow Basics
docs.text: Workflows model deterministic and event-driven orchestration.

docs.title: 12.4 Safety Model
docs.text: Constrain tool permissions and validate outputs before action.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Agent retrieves policy context then invokes approval tool for escalation.

docs.title: 13.1 Observability Baseline
docs.text: Track traces, retrieval metrics, tool latency, and error rates.

docs.title: 13.2 Evaluation Metrics
docs.text: Measure faithfulness, relevance, correctness, and citation quality.

docs.title: 13.3 Testing Layers
docs.text: Use unit, integration, and scenario-level agent tests.

docs.title: 13.4 Release Gates
docs.text: Block deployment when quality or safety thresholds regress.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Regression suite detects retrieval drift after embedding model update.

docs.title: 14.1 Serving Models
docs.text: Deploy as API services, scheduled pipelines, or interactive applications.

docs.title: 14.2 Infrastructure Patterns
docs.text: Use containerized services with autoscaling and observability hooks.

docs.title: 14.3 Operational Controls
docs.text: Apply canary releases, rollback automation, and incident playbooks.

docs.title: 14.4 Data Refresh Strategy
docs.text: Coordinate ingestion refresh cycles with deployment windows.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: RAG service rolls out weekly index refreshes with staged canary validation.

docs.title: 15 - Labs, Projects, Glossary, and Operational Checklists
Lab 1
docs.text: Build ingestion and indexing pipeline from mixed document sources.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Create hybrid retriever plus query router with source-aware synthesis.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Build tool-enabled agent workflow with memory and safety checks.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Add evaluation harness and production readiness gates.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• node
  • retriever
  • query engine
  • workflow
  • groundedness

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• retrieval quality validated
  • safety and policy checks active
  • telemetry dashboards configured
  • rollback runbooks tested

docs.title: 16 - Advanced Ingestion Pipelines, Connectors, and Multimodal Parsing
16.1 Connector Strategy
docs.text: Design connector layers for enterprise APIs, SaaS systems, and cloud data sources.

docs.title: 16.2 Multimodal Parsing
docs.text: Ingest text, tables, images, and structured artifacts with modality-aware pipelines.

docs.title: 16.3 Incremental Freshness
docs.text: Use CDC and delta ingestion to maintain fresh indexes efficiently.

docs.title: 16.4 Quality Governance
docs.text: Add schema validation and provenance checks at each pipeline stage.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Knowledge platform ingests ticket text and screenshot evidence into one unified retrieval corpus.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Index Composition, Hybrid Retrieval, and Fusion
17.1 Composite Index Design
docs.text: Combine vector, keyword, and structured indexes for robust retrieval coverage.

docs.title: 17.2 Hybrid Retrieval
docs.text: Blend lexical and semantic scoring to reduce misses on exact and meaning-based queries.

docs.title: 17.3 Fusion Techniques
docs.text: Use weighted fusion and reranking to optimize final relevance.

docs.title: 17.4 Operational Tradeoffs
docs.text: Tune precision/recall against latency and cost budgets.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Policy assistant improves factual accuracy by fusing BM25 and vector retrieval results.

docs.title: 18.1 Agentic RAG Model
docs.text: Agent dynamically decides when to retrieve, re-retrieve, and verify evidence.

docs.title: 18.2 Workflow Orchestration
docs.text: Use LlamaIndex workflows to structure retrieve-plan-synthesize-validate loops.

docs.title: 18.3 Grounding Controls
docs.text: Require citation coverage and confidence thresholds before final answer.

docs.title: 18.4 Failure Recovery
docs.text: Fallback to alternate retrievers when evidence confidence is low.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support agent re-runs retrieval when first-pass sources fail policy confidence criteria.

docs.title: 19 - Advanced Multi-Agent Systems and Task Delegation
19.1 Topology Patterns
docs.text: Use supervisor-worker, committee, and hierarchical delegation models.

docs.title: 19.2 Delegation Contracts
docs.text: Pass structured context, confidence, and ownership metadata between agents.

docs.title: 19.3 Governance Controls
docs.text: Limit delegation depth and enforce domain-specific permissions.

docs.title: 19.4 Failure Isolation
docs.text: Contain sub-agent failures with fallback and escalation strategies.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Research coordinator delegates retrieval, validation, and synthesis to specialized agents.

docs.title: 20 - Advanced Tool Calling, Function Agents, and Structured Outputs
20.1 Tool Calling Strategy
docs.text: Define strict function schemas and execution contracts for agent actions.

docs.title: 20.2 Structured Outputs
docs.text: Use typed outputs for downstream automation reliability.

docs.title: 20.3 Safety and Policy
docs.text: Validate arguments and require approval for high-risk side effects.

docs.title: 20.4 Reliability Patterns
docs.text: Apply retries, idempotency, and error classification for robust execution.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Order assistant outputs validated JSON contract directly into fulfillment workflow.

docs.title: 21.1 Context Budgeting
docs.text: Prioritize high-signal evidence under token and latency limits.

docs.title: 21.2 Compression Methods
docs.text: Use summarization, deduplication, and salience scoring.

docs.title: 21.3 Caching Strategy
docs.text: Cache retrieval and synthesis artifacts with freshness controls.

docs.title: 21.4 Drift Management
docs.text: Revalidate cached outputs when source data changes.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support workflow caches common policy snippets and compresses long ticket history.

docs.title: 22 - Advanced Memory, Personalization, and Session Design
22.1 Session Architecture
docs.text: Separate thread-local context from persistent user memory.

docs.title: 22.2 Personalization Controls
docs.text: Use profile memory safely with explicit policy boundaries.

docs.title: 22.3 Lifecycle Policies
docs.text: Define capture, update, expiry, and deletion rules.

docs.title: 22.4 Multi-Tenant Safety
docs.text: Isolate memory per tenant and role with strict access controls.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer assistant personalizes recommendations while enforcing retention rules.

docs.title: 23.1 Tenant Isolation
docs.text: Partition indexes, memory, and tool scopes by tenant boundary.

docs.title: 23.2 Access Controls
docs.text: Apply role-based and attribute-based policies across retrieval and actions.

docs.title: 23.3 Governance Framework
docs.text: Version prompts, tool policies, and release controls.

docs.title: 23.4 Audit Readiness
docs.text: Log retrieval lineage and action approvals for compliance.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Enterprise deployment blocks cross-tenant retrieval through namespace-enforced policies.

docs.title: 24 - Performance Tuning, Latency, Cost, and Throughput
24.1 Performance Model
docs.text: Measure retrieval latency, model latency, orchestration overhead, and queue time.

docs.title: 24.2 Cost Optimization
docs.text: Tune top-k, reranking depth, model routing, and caching policy.

docs.title: 24.3 Throughput Engineering
docs.text: Parallelize independent retrieval/tool tasks and control backpressure.

docs.title: 24.4 Optimization Loop
docs.text: Use telemetry-driven iterative tuning with rollback safety.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team reduces p95 latency by parallel retrieval and adaptive reranking limits.

docs.title: 25 - Advanced Evaluation: Faithfulness, Relevance, and Regression Gates
25.1 Evaluation Dimensions
docs.text: Assess faithfulness, relevance, completeness, and citation quality.

docs.title: 25.2 Dataset Strategy
docs.text: Build domain-specific benchmark sets with hard negative cases.

docs.title: 25.3 Regression Gates
docs.text: Gate deployments on statistically significant quality thresholds.

docs.title: 25.4 Continuous Validation
docs.text: Monitor online drift and retrain/reconfigure when quality degrades.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Release pipeline blocks rollout after regression test detects faithfulness drop.

docs.title: 26 - Observability, Tracing, Incident Response, and SLOs
26.1 Traceability
docs.text: Instrument end-to-end traces across retrieval, tools, and synthesis.

docs.title: 26.2 SLO Framework
docs.text: Define SLOs for quality, latency, availability, and safety outcomes.

docs.title: 26.3 Incident Operations
docs.text: Prepare runbooks for retrieval failure, model outage, and policy incidents.

docs.title: 26.4 Postmortem Discipline
docs.text: Document root causes and preventive actions after incidents.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: On-call team isolates failing reranker dependency and restores SLA through fallback mode.

docs.title: 27 - Advanced Deployment Patterns: Kubernetes, Serverless, and Edge
27.1 Deployment Topologies
docs.text: Choose Kubernetes, serverless, or edge patterns by latency and governance requirements.

docs.title: 27.2 Scaling Design
docs.text: Scale ingestion, retrieval, and synthesis services independently.

docs.title: 27.3 Release Engineering
docs.text: Use canary, blue/green, and fast rollback strategies.

docs.title: 27.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Add health checks, retries, and regional failover support.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Global assistant serves low-latency retrieval from regional edge nodes.

docs.title: 28 - LlamaIndex with MCP, A2A, and External Orchestrators
28.1 Interoperability Goal
docs.text: Combine LlamaIndex retrieval strengths with protocol-driven tool and agent ecosystems.

docs.title: 28.2 MCP Integration
docs.text: Use MCP to expose tools/resources through standardized contracts.

docs.title: 28.3 A2A and Orchestration
docs.text: Connect LlamaIndex agents with external agents and workflow orchestrators.

docs.title: 28.4 Security Boundaries
docs.text: Enforce scoped capabilities and audited cross-system interactions.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Coordinator agent delegates via A2A and queries enterprise knowledge via LlamaIndex retrieval.

docs.title: 29 - Migration, Versioning, and Ecosystem Compatibility Strategy
29.1 Version Strategy
docs.text: Track framework and integration package changes with compatibility mapping.

docs.title: 29.2 Migration Playbook
docs.text: Use staged rollout, shadow traffic, and rollback checkpoints.

docs.title: 29.3 Contract Stability
docs.text: Version prompts, tool schemas, and retrieval configurations.

docs.title: 29.4 Risk Mitigation
docs.text: Freeze high-risk changes during migration windows.

docs.title: 29.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team upgrades retrieval stack while preserving production SLOs through dual-path validation.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: Design a Production LlamaIndex Platform
30.1 Capstone Goal
docs.text: Design a production-grade LlamaIndex platform with retrieval, agents, and governance controls.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text: Architecture diagram, ingestion/retrieval design, evaluation framework, and operational SLOs.

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text: Assess reliability, faithfulness, scalability, and compliance readiness.

docs.title: 30.4 Failure Simulation
docs.text: Include outage, rollback, and post-incident improvement plan.

docs.title: 30.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Capstone team ships enterprise assistant with governed retrieval and auditable tool workflows.
