docs.title: 1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build complete production-ready LLMOps capability from experimentation to continuous operations.

docs.title: 1.2 Lifecycle
docs.text: Plan -> build -> evaluate -> deploy -> observe -> improve.

docs.title: 1.3 Core Domains
docs.text: DataOps, PromptOps, ModelOps, EvalOps, and ReliabilityOps.

docs.title: 1.4 Success Criteria
docs.text: Reliable quality, controlled cost, strong safety, and rapid iteration.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support assistant evolves weekly through tracked evaluations and safe canary deployments.

docs.title: 2.1 Environment Design
docs.text: Use isolated dev, staging, and production environments with strict configuration boundaries.

docs.title: 2.2 Platform Components
docs.text: Inference gateway, retrieval services, model registry, telemetry stack, and policy engine.

docs.title: 2.3 Secrets and Access
docs.text: Store credentials in managed secret systems and enforce least privilege.

docs.title: 2.4 Reproducibility Baseline
docs.text: Pin dependencies and version runtime configuration.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team validates every prompt/model change in staging before production promotion.

docs.title: 3.1 Artifact Taxonomy
docs.text: Track datasets, prompts, model configs, retrieval configs, and evaluation assets.

docs.title: 3.2 Version Strategy
docs.text: Use immutable versions and explicit promotion states.

docs.title: 3.3 Lineage Tracking
docs.text: Link outputs to exact data/prompt/model versions for auditability.

docs.title: 3.4 Governance
docs.text: Require review gates before promoting high-impact artifacts.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Bug in customer responses is traced to one prompt version and rolled back quickly.

docs.title: 4.1 Tracking Scope
docs.text: Record metrics, prompts, datasets, model parameters, and runtime context.

docs.title: 4.2 Reproducibility Goal
docs.text: Recreate any result exactly for debugging and compliance.

docs.title: 4.3 Comparison Workflow
docs.text: Compare experiment runs by quality, cost, and latency dimensions.

docs.title: 4.4 Decision Criteria
docs.text: Promote only experiments meeting target thresholds.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team selects new configuration after tracked run improves faithfulness without cost increase.

docs.title: 5.1 Registry Role
docs.text: Centralize model artifacts, metadata, and lifecycle states.

docs.title: 5.2 Lineage Model
docs.text: Map model versions to datasets, prompts, evaluations, and deployments.

docs.title: 5.3 Governance Controls
docs.text: Enforce approval workflows and release criteria per model tier.

docs.title: 5.4 Risk Management
docs.text: Tag models by risk class and allowed production scope.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: High-risk model requires additional security review before production deployment.

docs.title: 6.1 Evaluation Scope
docs.text: Measure correctness, faithfulness, relevance, safety, and robustness.

docs.title: 6.2 Benchmark Datasets
docs.text: Create fixed datasets with representative and adversarial scenarios.

docs.title: 6.3 Metric Strategy
docs.text: Combine automatic metrics with human-reviewed slices for high-risk domains.

docs.title: 6.4 Regression Detection
docs.text: Flag quality drops before release.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Offline benchmark catches drop in citation accuracy after retriever change.

docs.title: 07 - Online Evaluation, A/B Testing, and Canaries
7.1 Online Metrics
docs.text: Track user satisfaction, task completion, latency, safety incidents, and cost per request.

docs.title: 7.2 A/B Design
docs.text: Compare variants with statistically valid traffic splits.

docs.title: 7.3 Canary Rollout
docs.text: Expose limited traffic before full deployment.

docs.title: 7.4 Rollback Triggers
docs.text: Define automated rollback thresholds for critical regressions.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Canary rollout halts when safety incident rate increases above baseline.

docs.title: 8.1 PromptOps Scope
docs.text: Treat prompts and context templates as governed production artifacts.

docs.title: 8.2 Configuration Layers
docs.text: Separate static defaults, environment overrides, and runtime controls.

docs.title: 8.3 Change Governance
docs.text: Require reviews, tests, and approvals before prompt promotion.

docs.title: 8.4 Drift Prevention
docs.text: Monitor prompt behavior changes with regression suites.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Prompt update passes staging tests, then is rolled out to 10% traffic first.

docs.title: 9.1 RAGOps Scope
docs.text: Operate ingestion, indexing, retrieval, reranking, and citation workflows.

docs.title: 9.2 Freshness Controls
docs.text: Define sync frequency and stale-data detection policies.

docs.title: 9.3 Quality Monitoring
docs.text: Track retrieval precision, coverage, and citation consistency.

docs.title: 9.4 Failure Handling
docs.text: Fallback to alternate retrievers or cached responses.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Policy index refresh is delayed; system routes to backup index with warning telemetry.

docs.title: 10.1 Pipeline Design
docs.text: Automate linting, tests, evals, security checks, and deployment gates.

docs.title: 10.2 Artifact Promotion
docs.text: Promote only signed and validated artifacts through environments.

docs.title: 10.3 Deployment Safety
docs.text: Use canary and blue/green rollout patterns.

docs.title: 10.4 Rollback Strategy
docs.text: Keep fast rollback path with preserved prior artifacts.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Pipeline blocks release after eval gate detects unacceptable hallucination increase.

docs.title: 11.1 Serving Models
docs.text: Deploy as synchronous APIs, async workers, or hybrid orchestration services.

docs.title: 11.2 Runtime Controls
docs.text: Set rate limits, timeouts, retries, and fallback routes.

docs.title: 11.3 Scalability
docs.text: Scale inference and retrieval layers independently.

docs.title: 11.4 Availability Design
docs.text: Use multi-region failover for critical workloads.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Inference gateway shifts traffic to secondary region during primary outage.

docs.title: 12.1 Signal Layers
docs.text: Capture request traces, model usage, tool calls, and policy events.

docs.title: 12.2 Correlation
docs.text: Link traces across retrieval, inference, and action pipelines.

docs.title: 12.3 Dashboards
docs.text: Visualize latency, cost, quality, and safety KPIs.

docs.title: 12.4 Alerting
docs.text: Trigger actionable alerts for SLO and policy breaches.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Trace analysis reveals slow reranker as root cause of p95 latency spike.

docs.title: 13.1 Safety Controls
docs.text: Apply content filters, refusal policies, and high-risk action checks.

docs.title: 13.2 Security Controls
docs.text: Enforce zero-trust access, secret rotation, and tool sandboxing.

docs.title: 13.3 Policy Engine
docs.text: Evaluate runtime context against policy-as-code rules.

docs.title: 13.4 Compliance Logging
docs.text: Record policy decisions and action outcomes.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Sensitive data exfiltration attempt is blocked by policy gate and alerted.

docs.title: 14 - Incident Response, SRE, and Reliability Engineering
14.1 Incident Lifecycle
docs.text: Detect, triage, contain, recover, and review.

docs.title: 14.2 Reliability Patterns
docs.text: Use retries, circuit breakers, and graceful degradation paths.

docs.title: 14.3 SRE Practices
docs.text: Define SLOs, error budgets, and runbook ownership.

docs.title: 14.4 Learning Loop
docs.text: Feed postmortem insights into tests and controls.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Model provider outage triggers fallback model and preserved service availability.

docs.title: 15 - Labs, Projects, Glossary, and Operational Checklists
Lab 1
docs.text: Build experiment-tracked prompt and model comparison workflow.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Create RAGOps pipeline with freshness and citation monitoring.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Implement CI/CD with eval gate, canary rollout, and rollback automation.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Set up observability dashboard and incident runbook simulation.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• PromptOps
  • EvalOps
  • RAGOps
  • model registry
  • error budget

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• quality gates passing
  • security policies enforced
  • observability complete
  • rollback tested

docs.title: 16.1 Routing Strategy
docs.text: Select models dynamically by intent complexity, risk, and SLA requirements.

docs.title: 16.2 Adaptive Orchestration
docs.text: Route steps across model tiers and tool chains using runtime feedback.

docs.title: 16.3 Governance Controls
docs.text: Enforce routing policies to avoid unsafe or high-cost paths.

docs.title: 16.4 Failure Handling
docs.text: Use fallback models and degraded operation modes.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Support workflow escalates legal queries to high-accuracy model only when needed.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Evaluation, Synthetic Data, and LLM Judges
17.1 Synthetic Data Strategy
docs.text: Generate targeted test cases for rare and high-risk failure modes.

docs.title: 17.2 LLM-as-Judge Patterns
docs.text: Use rubric-based judging with calibration against human gold labels.

docs.title: 17.3 Bias and Drift Checks
docs.text: Continuously audit evaluator drift and label consistency.

docs.title: 17.4 Release Criteria
docs.text: Combine judge scores with deterministic policy checks.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Synthetic adversarial prompts reveal jailbreak vulnerability before release.

docs.title: 18 - Cost Engineering, Token Economics, and Budgets
18.1 Cost Model
docs.text: Break down spend by model usage, retrieval, tools, and infrastructure.

docs.title: 18.2 Budget Controls
docs.text: Set tenant, workflow, and environment-level budget guardrails.

docs.title: 18.3 Token Optimization
docs.text: Reduce waste via prompt compression and targeted context inclusion.

docs.title: 18.4 FinOps Reporting
docs.text: Track marginal cost per outcome and per user segment.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Token optimization lowers monthly spend while preserving quality targets.

docs.title: 19.1 Latency Profiling
docs.text: Measure per-stage latency: retrieval, model inference, tool calls, synthesis.

docs.title: 19.2 Throughput Patterns
docs.text: Use parallel branches and queue tuning for high concurrency.

docs.title: 19.3 Bottleneck Mitigation
docs.text: Optimize slow tools, retrieval fan-out, and model response budgets.

docs.title: 19.4 SLO Alignment
docs.text: Tune systems to meet p95 latency and availability targets.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Parallel retrieval reduces response time by 40% under peak load.

docs.title: 20 - Caching, Context Compression, and Response Acceleration
20.1 Caching Layers
docs.text: Cache retrieval results, tool outputs, and stable response fragments.

docs.title: 20.2 Context Compression
docs.text: Summarize and rank context to fit high-value evidence in limited windows.

docs.title: 20.3 Freshness Policies
docs.text: Invalidate caches based on source updates and risk sensitivity.

docs.title: 20.4 Tradeoff Management
docs.text: Balance speed gains against stale-context risk.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Policy lookup cache accelerates responses while refreshing on document change events.

docs.title: 21 - Human Feedback Loops and Continuous Learning
21.1 Feedback Sources
docs.text: Capture explicit ratings, corrections, and escalation outcomes.

docs.title: 21.2 Triage Workflow
docs.text: Classify feedback into quality, safety, and usability categories.

docs.title: 21.3 Learning Pipeline
docs.text: Convert high-signal feedback into prompt, retrieval, and model updates.

docs.title: 21.4 Governance
docs.text: Require review before applying feedback-driven production changes.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Agent improves ticket summary accuracy after incorporating reviewer corrections.

docs.title: 22 - Data Flywheel and Continuous Improvement Systems
22.1 Flywheel Model
docs.text: Usage telemetry and feedback continuously improve data, prompts, and models.

docs.title: 22.2 Prioritization
docs.text: Rank improvement backlog by impact and risk reduction.

docs.title: 22.3 Experiment Loop
docs.text: Deploy incremental changes with controlled evaluation and rollback.

docs.title: 22.4 Organizational Alignment
docs.text: Tie flywheel outputs to product and SRE planning cycles.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Weekly flywheel iteration reduces unsupported answers in compliance workflow.

docs.title: 23 - Compliance, Auditability, and Policy as Code
23.1 Policy-as-Code
docs.text: Encode legal and internal controls as executable runtime rules.

docs.title: 23.2 Auditability
docs.text: Maintain immutable logs for decisions, approvals, and data usage.

docs.title: 23.3 Evidence Collection
docs.text: Generate compliance artifacts automatically during workflow execution.

docs.title: 23.4 Regulatory Mapping
docs.text: Map controls to applicable standards and jurisdictional requirements.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Audit report reconstructs full decision path for high-risk agent action.

docs.title: 24.1 Tenant Isolation
docs.text: Segment data, models, prompts, and tool credentials by tenant boundary.

docs.title: 24.2 Control Plane
docs.text: Manage tenant-specific policies, budgets, and evaluation gates.

docs.title: 24.3 Scalability
docs.text: Share infrastructure safely while preserving tenant guarantees.

docs.title: 24.4 Compliance
docs.text: Support regional data residency and tenant-specific governance needs.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Platform prevents cross-tenant context leakage under shared inference infrastructure.

docs.title: 25 - AgentOps for Tool Calling and Autonomous Workflows
25.1 AgentOps Scope
docs.text: Operate planner-executor, tool-using, and delegated agent workflows safely.

docs.title: 25.2 Control Mechanisms
docs.text: Use interrupts, approval gates, and policy filters for autonomous steps.

docs.title: 25.3 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Bound loops, set retry budgets, and track execution trajectories.

docs.title: 25.4 Observability
docs.text: Capture node-level traces for reasoning and action outcomes.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Autonomous remediation flow pauses before infrastructure-changing command.

docs.title: 26 - Multimodal LLMOps: Text, Image, Audio, and Video
26.1 Modality Strategy
docs.text: Design pipelines for ingestion, indexing, and evaluation across modalities.

docs.title: 26.2 Data Operations
docs.text: Track multimodal artifacts with consistent metadata and lineage.

docs.title: 26.3 Quality Controls
docs.text: Evaluate modality-specific accuracy and cross-modal consistency.

docs.title: 26.4 Runtime Considerations
docs.text: Optimize bandwidth, storage, and inference costs for heavy media workloads.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer support agent analyzes screenshots and chat logs in one workflow.

docs.title: 27 - ProtocolOps: MCP, A2A, and Interoperability Governance
27.1 ProtocolOps Purpose
docs.text: Operationalize cross-system interoperability with standard protocol contracts.

docs.title: 27.2 MCP Governance
docs.text: Manage tool/resource contracts, capability scopes, and runtime policy checks.

docs.title: 27.3 A2A Governance
docs.text: Control agent-to-agent delegation with trust and audit boundaries.

docs.title: 27.4 Observability
docs.text: Track protocol failures, compatibility drift, and policy violations.

28.1 Reference Stack
docs.text: Ingress -> orchestration -> retrieval/tools -> model gateway -> governance/observability.

docs.title: 28.2 Integration Patterns
docs.text: Connect CRM, ERP, ticketing, data lakehouse, and event bus systems.

docs.title: 28.3 Control Plane
docs.text: Centralize policy, release, and telemetry governance.

docs.title: 28.4 Domain Patterns
docs.text: Adapt architecture for support, finance, healthcare, and operations.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example

docs.text: Manage compatible evolution of prompts, models, retrievers, and policies.

docs.text: This folder contains a complete Foundations + Advanced documentation set.

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
1. Foundations Track: chapters 01-15
  2. Advanced Track: chapters 16-30

docs.title: 01 - Program Overview and Linear Algebra for AI
1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build linear algebra intuition and implementation skills required for modern AI models.

docs.title: 1.2 Why It Matters
docs.text: AI operations are mostly linear algebra: - feature transformations - neural network layers - optimization steps - embedding similarity search

docs.title: 1.3 Learning Path
docs.text:
1. Vector and matrix fundamentals
  2. Solvers and decompositions
  3. Spectral and dimensionality methods
  4. Advanced numerical and large-scale AI systems

docs.title: 1.4 Outcomes
docs.text:
• understand model math
  • debug numerical issues
  • optimize matrix-heavy pipelines

docs.title: 2.1 Core Objects
docs.text:
• scalar: single value
  • vector: ordered 1D values
  • matrix: 2D array
  • tensor: n-dimensional array

docs.title: 2.2 Shapes and Dimensions
docs.text: Always track shape. Most bugs in AI pipelines are shape mismatches.

docs.title: 2.3 Indexing Notation
docs.text:
• vector: x_i
  • matrix: A_{ij}
  • tensor: T_{ijk...}

docs.title: 2.4 Practical Rule
docs.text: Write expected input and output shapes for every model component.

docs.title: 3.1 Core Operations
docs.text:
• addition/subtraction
  • scalar multiplication
  • matrix multiplication
  • transpose

docs.title: 3.2 Multiplication Rules
docs.text: If A is (m x n) and B is (n x p), AB is (m x p).

docs.title: 3.3 Key Properties
docs.text:
• associativity: (AB)C = A(BC)
  • distributivity: A(B+C)=AB+AC
  • generally not commutative: AB != BA

docs.title: 3.4 Identity and Zero Matrices
docs.text: Use identity I and zero matrix for algebraic simplification.

docs.title: 4.1 Linear Combination
docs.text: A vector formed by weighted sum of vectors.

docs.title: 4.2 Span
docs.text: Set of all vectors reachable by linear combinations of a set.

docs.title: 4.3 Linear Independence
docs.text: Vectors are independent if none can be written as combination of others.

docs.title: 4.4 Basis
docs.text: A minimal independent set spanning a vector space.

docs.title: 4.5 Dimension
docs.text: Number of basis vectors.

docs.title: 4.6 AI Relevance
docs.text: Feature representation quality depends on basis and dimensional structure.

docs.title: 5.1 System Form
docs.text: Ax = b represents multiple linear equations.

docs.title: 5.2 Row Operations
docs.text:
• swap rows
  • scale row
  • add multiple of one row to another

docs.title: 5.3 Row Echelon Forms
docs.text: Use elimination to reach upper-triangular or reduced row echelon form.

docs.title: 5.4 Solution Cases
docs.text:
• unique solution
  • infinitely many solutions
  • no solution

docs.title: 5.5 AI Relevance
docs.text: Linear solvers appear in least squares and many optimization subproblems.

docs.title: 6.1 Rank
docs.text: Number of independent columns (or rows) in matrix A.

docs.title: 6.2 Null Space
docs.text: Set of vectors x such that Ax = 0.

docs.title: 6.3 Nullity
docs.text: Dimension of null space.

docs.title: 6.4 Rank-Nullity Theorem
docs.text: For A with n columns: rank(A) + nullity(A) = n

docs.title: 6.5 Practical Impact
docs.text: Low rank often indicates redundant features or compressible representations.

docs.title: 7.1 Determinant Meaning
docs.text: det(A) measures volume scaling of transformation A.

docs.title: 7.2 Invertibility Test
docs.text: det(A) != 0 implies matrix is invertible.

docs.title: 7.3 Significance of Sign
docs.text: Sign reflects orientation flip or preservation.

docs.title: 7.4 Practical Note
docs.text: Determinants are often numerically unstable for large matrices; prefer decomposition-based checks.

docs.title: 7.5 AI Relevance
docs.text: Log-determinants appear in probabilistic models and covariance computations.

docs.title: 8.1 Matrix Inverse
docs.text: A^{-1} satisfies AA^{-1} = I.

docs.title: 8.2 Solving Systems
docs.text: x = A^{-1}b mathematically, but in practice use decomposition solvers.

docs.title: 8.3 Why Not Explicit Inverse
docs.text: Explicit inversion is slower and less stable than solving linear systems directly.

docs.title: 8.4 Decomposition Alternatives
docs.text:
• LU decomposition
  • QR decomposition
  • Cholesky for SPD matrices

docs.title: 8.5 AI Relevance
docs.text: Stable solvers are critical in regression and Kalman-style systems.

docs.title: 9.1 Common Norms
docs.text:
• L1 norm
  • L2 norm
  • infinity norm

docs.title: 9.2 Distance Metrics
docs.text: Distance between vectors often uses L2 or L1 depending on task geometry.

docs.title: 9.3 Cosine Similarity
docs.text: Measures angle-based similarity: cos(theta) = (x . y) / (||x|| ||y||)

docs.title: 9.4 AI Applications
docs.text:
• nearest-neighbor retrieval
  • embedding search
  • clustering

docs.title: 9.5 Practical Rule
docs.text: Normalize vectors before cosine-based retrieval.

docs.title: 10.1 Orthogonality
docs.text: Vectors are orthogonal if dot product is zero.

docs.title: 10.2 Projection
docs.text: Project vector x onto subspace basis to isolate explainable component.

docs.title: 10.3 Gram-Schmidt
docs.text: Transforms independent vectors into orthonormal basis.

docs.title: 10.4 QR Decomposition
docs.text: Gram-Schmidt underlies QR methods used in stable solving and least squares.

docs.title: 10.5 AI Relevance
docs.text: Orthogonal components improve interpretability and numerical behavior.

docs.title: 11.1 Definition
docs.text: For matrix A, eigenpair satisfies: Av = lambda v

docs.title: 11.2 Interpretation
docs.text: Eigenvectors are invariant directions; eigenvalues are scaling factors.

docs.title: 11.3 Diagonalization
docs.text: If enough independent eigenvectors exist: A = PDP^{-1}

docs.title: 11.4 AI Uses
docs.text:
• PCA
  • spectral clustering
  • stability analysis

docs.title: 11.5 Practical Note
docs.text: Symmetric matrices have real eigenvalues and orthogonal eigenvectors.

docs.title: 12 - Singular Value Decomposition and Low-Rank Approximation
12.1 SVD Form
docs.text: A = U Sigma V^T

docs.title: 12.2 Singular Values
docs.text: Diagonal entries of Sigma show strength of principal directions.

docs.title: 12.3 Low-Rank Approximation
docs.text: Keep top-k singular values/vectors to compress matrix with minimal error.

docs.title: 12.4 Applications
docs.text:
• dimensionality reduction
  • recommendation systems
  • denoising
  • compression

docs.title: 12.5 AI Relevance
docs.text: SVD explains latent structure in large feature matrices.

docs.title: 13 - Positive Definite Matrices and Quadratic Forms
13.1 Quadratic Form
docs.text: x^T A x describes curvature geometry.

docs.title: 13.2 Positive Definite (PD)
docs.text: Matrix A is PD if x^T A x > 0 for all nonzero x.

docs.title: 13.3 Positive Semidefinite (PSD)
docs.text: x^T A x >= 0.

docs.title: 13.4 Optimization Link
docs.text: Hessian PSD/PD properties determine convexity and optimization stability.

docs.title: 13.5 AI Use Cases
docs.text:
• covariance matrices
  • kernel matrices
  • second-order optimization analysis

docs.title: Lab 1
docs.text: Implement Gaussian elimination and compare with library solver outputs.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Build PCA pipeline and visualize explained variance tradeoff.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Use SVD for low-rank image compression and measure reconstruction error.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Compute cosine similarity search over embedding vectors.

docs.title: Real-Time Example
docs.text: Recommendation feature matrix: 1. clean and center data 2. apply truncated SVD 3. build low-rank user/item factors 4. evaluate ranking quality

docs.title: Capstone Starter
docs.text: Design matrix pipeline for one real AI problem and document numerical risks.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• rank
  • null space
  • orthonormal basis
  • condition number
  • eigendecomposition
  • SVD
  • PSD/PD

docs.title: Math Review Template
docs.text:
1. Definitions
  2. Key formulas
  3. Geometric interpretation
  4. AI use case

docs.title: Project Checklist
docs.text:
• shape checks complete
  • solver stability reviewed
  • decomposition choice justified
  • metrics and error analysis included

docs.title: 16.1 Why Matrix Calculus
docs.text: Deep learning training is gradient computation over matrix/tensor operations.

docs.title: 16.2 Core Derivatives
docs.text:
• gradient of dot products
  • Jacobians for vector functions
  • chain rule for composed layers

docs.title: 16.3 Backpropagation View
docs.text: Backprop reuses intermediate derivatives from output to input efficiently.

docs.title: 16.4 Practical Rule
docs.text: Keep tensor shape annotations for each derivative to avoid silent gradient bugs.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Derive gradients for linear layer: y = Wx + b.

docs.title: 17.1 Condition Number
docs.text: kappa(A) measures sensitivity of solution to perturbations.

docs.title: 17.2 Ill-Conditioning
docs.text: High condition number causes amplified numerical error.

docs.title: 17.3 Geometry
docs.text: Elongated loss contours slow gradient descent.

docs.title: 17.4 Mitigations
docs.text:
• feature scaling
  • preconditioning
  • regularization

docs.title: 17.5 AI Relevance
docs.text: Conditioning impacts training speed and reliability.

docs.title: 18 - PCA, Spectral Methods, and Dimensionality Reduction
18.1 PCA Recap
docs.text: Find orthogonal directions maximizing variance.

docs.title: 18.2 Spectral Methods
docs.text: Use eigenvectors/eigenvalues of matrices (covariance, graph Laplacian) for structure discovery.

docs.title: 18.3 Dimensionality Reduction Tradeoff
docs.text: Lower dimension reduces noise and cost but may lose signal.

docs.title: 18.4 Selection Strategy
docs.text: Pick components by explained variance and downstream model performance.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Reduce 2048-d embeddings to 256-d for faster retrieval with minimal recall loss.

docs.title: 19.1 Motivation
docs.text: Approximate methods reduce cost on large matrices.

docs.title: 19.2 Core Techniques
docs.text:
• random projections
  • sketching
  • randomized SVD

docs.title: 19.3 Guarantees
docs.text: Randomized methods can preserve geometry with controllable error bounds.

docs.title: 19.4 AI Use Cases
docs.text:
• fast approximate nearest neighbors
  • low-rank approximation at scale
  • large covariance approximations

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Use randomized SVD to compress massive user-item matrix quickly.

docs.title: 20 - Sparse Linear Algebra and Compressed Representations
20.1 Sparse Matrices
docs.text: Most values are zero; exploit sparsity for memory and speed.

docs.title: 20.2 Storage Formats
docs.text:
• CSR
  • CSC
  • COO

docs.title: 20.3 Sparse Operations
docs.text: Sparse-dense multiplication is central in recommendation and graph workloads.

docs.title: 20.4 Compression Benefits
docs.text:
• lower memory footprint
  • faster I/O
  • scalable model serving

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Serve large one-hot feature interactions using sparse matrix pipeline.

docs.title: 21 - Numerical Linear Algebra and Floating Point Error
21.1 Floating-Point Reality
docs.text: Real arithmetic in computers is approximate.

docs.title: 21.2 Error Sources
docs.text:
• rounding
  • cancellation
  • overflow/underflow

docs.title: 21.3 Stable Algorithms
docs.text: Choose numerically stable decompositions over naive formulas.

docs.title: 21.4 Precision Choices
docs.text:
• float32 for speed
  • float64 for stability-sensitive tasks
  • mixed precision with safeguards

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Training divergence traced to unstable normalization in float16 path.

docs.title: 22 - Iterative Solvers and Krylov Subspace Methods
22.1 Why Iterative Solvers
docs.text: For very large systems, direct factorization is expensive.

docs.title: 22.2 Common Methods
docs.text:
• gradient descent for least squares
  • conjugate gradient (SPD systems)
  • GMRES/BiCGSTAB for general systems

docs.title: 22.3 Preconditioning
docs.text: Good preconditioners drastically improve convergence speed.

docs.title: 22.4 Stopping Criteria
docs.text: Use residual thresholds and max iteration limits.

docs.title: 22.5 AI Relevance
docs.text: Large-scale optimization and inverse problems rely on iterative methods.

docs.title: 23.1 Tensor Operations
docs.text:
• mode products
  • tensor contractions
  • reshaping/unfolding

docs.title: 23.2 Decompositions
docs.text:
• CP decomposition
  • Tucker decomposition
  • Tensor Train

docs.title: 23.3 Applications
docs.text:
• multimodal learning
  • recommender systems
  • compression of deep models

docs.title: 23.4 Practical Consideration
docs.text: Tensor rank selection controls tradeoff between accuracy and complexity.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Compress 3D interaction tensor for recommendation inference speedup.

docs.title: 24 - Graph Linear Algebra and GNN Foundations
24.1 Graph Matrices
docs.text:
• adjacency matrix
  • degree matrix
  • Laplacian matrix

docs.title: 24.2 Spectral Graph Concepts
docs.text: Laplacian eigenvectors capture graph structure for clustering and embedding.

docs.title: 24.3 Message Passing as Linear Algebra
docs.text: GNN propagation uses repeated neighborhood aggregation via sparse matrix operations.

docs.title: 24.4 Stability
docs.text: Normalization of adjacency/Laplacian helps stable training.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Node classification pipeline using sparse adjacency multiplication.

docs.title: 25 - Kernel Methods and Feature Space Geometry
25.1 Kernel Idea
docs.text: Compute inner products in implicit high-dimensional feature spaces.

docs.title: 25.2 Kernel Matrix
docs.text: Gram matrix K must be PSD for valid kernel methods.

docs.title: 25.3 Common Kernels
docs.text:
• linear
  • polynomial
  • RBF

docs.title: 25.4 Scalability Challenge
docs.text: Kernel methods scale poorly with data size; approximations are often required.

docs.title: 25.5 AI Relevance
docs.text: Kernel geometry offers strong non-linear decision boundaries in moderate-size datasets.

docs.title: 26 - Distributed and GPU Linear Algebra for AI
26.1 Parallelism Modes
docs.text:
• data parallel matrix ops
  • model parallel tensor ops
  • pipeline parallel training

docs.title: 26.2 GPU Efficiency
docs.text: Performance depends on memory bandwidth, kernel fusion, and batch sizing.

docs.title: 26.3 Distributed Tradeoffs
docs.text: Communication overhead can dominate if workload partitioning is poor.

docs.title: 26.4 Practical Controls
docs.text:
• mixed precision
  • gradient accumulation
  • overlapping communication with compute

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Scale training job across multi-GPU setup while keeping numerical stability checks.

docs.title: 27 - Linear Algebra in Deep Learning Architectures
27.1 Dense Layers
docs.text: Affine transformations dominate MLP computation.

docs.title: 27.2 Convolutions
docs.text: Convolution can be expressed as structured matrix multiplication.

docs.title: 27.3 Attention
docs.text: Self-attention is matrix multiplication plus softmax weighting.

docs.title: 27.4 Normalization Layers
docs.text: Batch/layer normalization involve matrix/tensor statistics.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Analyze transformer block compute cost through Q/K/V matrix multiplications.

docs.title: 28 - Embeddings, LLM Geometry, and Vector Databases
28.1 Embedding Spaces
docs.text: Embeddings map items to dense vector spaces where geometry captures semantic similarity.

docs.title: 28.2 Similarity Metrics
docs.text:
• cosine similarity
  • dot product
  • Euclidean distance

docs.title: 28.3 Indexing for Retrieval
docs.text: Vector databases use ANN structures for fast nearest-neighbor search.

docs.title: 28.4 Quality Considerations
docs.text:
• normalization consistency
  • embedding drift monitoring
  • index refresh strategy

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: RAG system retrieves top-k document chunks by cosine similarity over embedding vectors.

docs.title: 29 - Reliability Diagnostics and Numerical Incident Response
29.1 Common Incidents
docs.text:
• exploding/vanishing values
  • NaN propagation
  • solver non-convergence
  • unstable gradients

docs.title: 29.2 Monitoring Signals
docs.text:
• gradient norm statistics
  • condition-number trend
  • NaN/Inf counters
  • residual and convergence logs

docs.title: 29.3 Response Runbook
docs.text:
1. detect and isolate
  2. rollback to last stable config
  3. patch numeric guardrails
  4. validate on benchmark suite

docs.title: 29.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Model training halted on NaN spike; root cause traced to mixed-precision overflow.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: Linear Algebra System Design
30.1 Objective
docs.text: Design a production-grade AI workflow with explicit linear algebra decisions and numerical safeguards.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text:
• mathematical design spec
  • decomposition and solver choices
  • stability/risk analysis
  • benchmark and performance report
  • incident and rollback plan

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text:
• correctness of mathematical approach
  • numerical stability and robustness
  • scalability and efficiency
  • clarity of documentation
  • operational readiness

docs.title: 30.4 Suggested Capstones
docs.text:
• large-scale recommendation factorization
  • embedding retrieval platform
  • graph-based prediction pipeline
  • low-rank compression for model serving

docs.title: Linear Algebra for AI
docs.text: This folder contains a complete Foundations + Advanced documentation set.

docs.title: Open First
docs.text: • INDEX.md

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
1. Foundations Track: chapters 01-15
  2. Advanced Track: chapters 16-30

docs.title: 1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build production-ready MCP knowledge to connect AI applications with external tools, resources, and workflows.

docs.title: 1.2 What MCP Enables
docs.text: Standardized integration between AI hosts and external systems.

docs.title: 1.3 Ecosystem Components
docs.text:
• documentation and concepts
  • specification revisions
  • SDKs
  • registry
  • developer tools like MCP Inspector

docs.title: 1.4 Learning Path
docs.text: foundations -> implementation -> security -> operations -> advanced platform design.

docs.title: 02 - MCP Architecture: Host, Client, Server Model
2.1 Participants
docs.text:
• Host: AI application coordinating integrations
  • Client: connection manager per server
  • Server: provider of tools/resources/prompts

docs.title: 2.2 Connection Model
docs.text: One host can run multiple MCP clients, each linked to one MCP server session.

docs.title: 2.3 Layers
docs.text:
• data layer (JSON-RPC protocol semantics)
  • transport layer (stdio or Streamable HTTP)

docs.title: 2.4 Practical Rule
docs.text: Treat each MCP server as an isolated capability boundary.

docs.title: 03 - Base Protocol: JSON-RPC Messages and Schemas
3.1 Message Types
docs.text:
• requests
  • responses (result or error)
  • notifications

docs.title: 3.2 Request/Response Rules
docs.text: Request IDs must be unique within session and must not be null.

docs.title: 3.3 Notifications
docs.text: One-way messages with no response expected.

docs.title: 3.4 Schema Usage
docs.text: MCP relies on JSON Schema (default 2020-12) for tool/resource/prompt contracts.

docs.title: 04 - Lifecycle: Initialization, Version, and Capability Negotiation
4.1 Lifecycle Phases
docs.text:
1. initialization
  2. operation
  3. shutdown

docs.title: 4.2 Initialization
docs.text: Client sends initialize; server responds with negotiated version and capabilities; client sends notifications/initialized.

docs.title: 4.3 Capability Negotiation
docs.text: Define which features are active for the session (tools/resources/prompts/roots/sampling/elicitation/etc.).

docs.title: 4.4 Versioning
docs.text: Protocol uses date-based versions (for example, 2025-11-25).

docs.title: 05 - Transports: STDIO and Streamable HTTP Basics
5.1 Supported Transports
docs.text:
• stdio for local process communication
  • Streamable HTTP for remote communication

docs.title: 5.2 Streamable HTTP Basics
docs.text: POST/GET patterns with optional SSE streaming support.

docs.title: 5.3 Security Baseline
docs.text: Validate Origin headers and bind local servers to localhost where possible.

docs.title: 5.4 Compatibility Note
docs.text: Streamable HTTP replaces older HTTP+SSE design from prior revisions.

docs.title: 06 - Server Primitives Overview: Tools, Resources, Prompts
6.1 Tools
docs.text: Executable actions models can invoke.

docs.title: 6.2 Resources
docs.text: Context data exposed via URI-based access.

docs.title: 6.3 Prompts
docs.text: Reusable user-controlled templates with arguments.

docs.title: 6.4 Discovery Model
docs.text: Clients discover primitives via */list operations and then invoke/read/get as needed.

docs.title: 7.1 Core Methods
docs.text:
• tools/list
  • tools/call
  • notifications/tools/list_changed

docs.title: 7.2 Tool Contracts
docs.text: Define name, description, inputSchema, and optional outputSchema.

docs.title: 7.3 Execution Safety
docs.text: Require user visibility and approval for high-impact actions.

docs.title: 7.4 Practical Rule
docs.text: Keep tools focused, typed, and side-effect-aware.

docs.title: 8.1 Core Methods
docs.text:
• resources/list
  • resources/read
  • resources/templates/list
  • optional resources/subscribe

docs.title: 8.2 Notifications
docs.text: notifications/resources/list_changed and notifications/resources/updated.

docs.title: 8.3 Metadata
docs.text: Use URI, MIME type, size, and annotations for prioritization and audience hints.

docs.title: 8.4 Practical Rule
docs.text: Design stable URI schemes and clear source provenance.

docs.title: 9.1 Core Methods
docs.text:
• prompts/list
  • prompts/get
  • notifications/prompts/list_changed

docs.title: 9.2 Prompt Model
docs.text: Prompts are user-controlled templates with structured argument schemas.

docs.title: 9.3 UI Patterns
docs.text: slash commands, command palette, and guided form-based prompt usage.

docs.title: 9.4 Practical Rule
docs.text: Use prompts to standardize high-value repeat workflows.

docs.title: 10 - Client Primitives: Roots, Sampling, and Elicitation
10.1 Roots
docs.text: Filesystem boundary hints shared by client (roots/list, list change notifications).

docs.title: 10.2 Sampling
docs.text: Servers can request model completion through client (sampling/createMessage).

docs.title: 10.3 Elicitation
docs.text: Servers request additional user info (elicitation/create) in form or URL mode.

docs.title: 10.4 Trust Model
docs.text: Human-in-the-loop controls should be available for sampling and elicitation.

docs.title: 11.1 Local Server Model
docs.text: Typically launched by host using stdio transport.

docs.title: 11.2 Setup Considerations
docs.text: process lifecycle, environment variables, permission scope, and root boundaries.

docs.title: 11.3 Security Considerations
docs.text: local servers are high-trust components and require careful source validation.

docs.title: 11.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: IDE host connects to local filesystem and git MCP servers for coding workflows.

docs.title: 12 - Connecting to Remote MCP Servers and Auth Basics
12.1 Remote Model
docs.text: Use Streamable HTTP with session and protocol headers.

docs.title: 12.2 Authorization Basics
docs.text: OAuth-based authorization is recommended for protected servers.

docs.title: 12.3 Discovery
docs.text: Client discovers auth server metadata via protected resource metadata mechanisms.

docs.title: 12.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Enterprise client connects to remote MCP server with bearer token flow.

docs.title: 13 - Building MCP Servers and Clients with SDKs
13.1 SDK Coverage
docs.text: Official SDKs support building both MCP servers and clients.

docs.title: 13.2 Build Steps
docs.text: choose SDK -> define capabilities -> implement primitives -> test with inspector.

docs.title: 13.3 Compatibility Discipline
docs.text: track protocol revision and supported capability set explicitly.

docs.title: 13.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Minimal server exposes one tool and one resource with schema validation.

docs.title: Lab 1
docs.text: Create local stdio MCP server with tools and resources.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Build MCP client that lists tools and executes controlled calls.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Add prompt templates and resource subscriptions.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Implement remote server auth flow and capability negotiation checks.

docs.title: Real-Time Example
docs.text: Customer-support assistant uses MCP server set for CRM, policy docs, and ticket tools.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• host
  • client
  • server
  • primitive
  • capability negotiation

docs.title: Server Spec Template
docs.text:
1. capabilities
  2. primitive contracts
  3. transport/auth design
  4. validation and monitoring plan

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• protocol compatibility verified
  • schema validation passes
  • security controls validated
  • rollback strategy documented

docs.title: 16 - Advanced Versioning, Capabilities, and Compatibility Strategy
16.1 Revision Strategy
docs.text: Use protocol revision dates with explicit compatibility checks at initialization.

docs.title: 16.2 Capability-First Contracts
docs.text: Treat capabilities as feature flags so clients and servers degrade gracefully.

docs.title: 16.3 Compatibility Matrix
docs.text: Track client revision x server revision x enabled capabilities in CI.

docs.title: 16.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: A desktop host supports older servers by disabling unsupported elicitation features.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Tools, Output Schemas, Annotations, and Safety
17.1 Strong Tool Contracts
docs.text: Define strict input and output schemas to reduce ambiguous model behavior.

docs.title: 17.2 Annotations and Hints
docs.text: Use tool annotations to communicate mutating behavior, latency expectations, and risk level.

docs.title: 17.3 Safety Controls
docs.text: Apply allowlists, argument validation, and side-effect confirmation for high-impact actions.

docs.title: 17.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: A payments tool requires explicit confirmation before transfer execution.

docs.title: 18 - Advanced Resources, URI Design, Updates, and Consistency
18.1 URI Architecture
docs.text: Design predictable URI namespaces by domain, tenancy, and data class.

docs.title: 18.2 Update Semantics
docs.text: Support explicit refresh and change notifications to avoid stale context.

docs.title: 18.3 Consistency Rules
docs.text: Define eventual vs strong consistency boundaries per resource type.

docs.title: 18.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Knowledge resources push updates after policy changes to keep assistants compliant.

docs.title: 19 - Advanced Prompts, Composition, and Parameter Completion
19.1 Prompt Composition
docs.text: Combine reusable prompt modules for role, policy, task, and output format.

docs.title: 19.2 Parameter Completion
docs.text: Use elicitation and defaults to complete missing variables safely.

docs.title: 19.3 Workflow Design
docs.text: Chain prompts with tool outputs and resource lookups for deterministic flows.

docs.title: 19.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Incident triage prompt auto-fills service metadata from monitored resources.

docs.title: 20 - Advanced Sampling, Model Preferences, and Tool Calling
20.1 Sampling Strategies
docs.text: Control temperature, stop conditions, and budget rules per task type.

docs.title: 20.2 Model Preferences
docs.text: Set routing preferences by capability, cost, and latency profiles.

docs.title: 20.3 Tool-Calling Governance
docs.text: Constrain recursive tool calls and enforce execution depth limits.

docs.title: 20.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: A support workflow routes extraction to a cheap model and escalation to a stronger model.

docs.title: 21 - Advanced Elicitation, Form/URL Modes, and Interaction Design
21.1 Elicitation Modes
docs.text: Use inline forms for quick inputs and URL mode for richer guided workflows.

docs.title: 21.2 Validation and UX
docs.text: Validate fields server-side and return actionable correction messages.

docs.title: 21.3 Human-in-the-Loop
docs.text: Design escalation paths when approvals or legal confirmations are required.

docs.title: 21.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: A deployment request opens a structured approval form before running a production tool.

docs.title: 22 - Advanced Transport, Sessions, Resumability, and Streaming
22.1 Session Management
docs.text: Use session identifiers to isolate and trace long-lived interactions.

docs.title: 22.2 Stream Resumability
docs.text: Resume interrupted streams with replay-safe event handling.

docs.title: 22.3 Backpressure and Reliability
docs.text: Apply heartbeat, timeout, and retry policies for stable delivery.

docs.title: 22.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Mobile client reconnects after network loss and resumes partial stream safely.

docs.title: 23 - Authorization Deep Dive: OAuth, PRM, PKCE, and Discovery
23.1 OAuth Roles and Flows
docs.text: Map MCP client and server responsibilities in delegated authorization.

docs.title: 23.2 PKCE and Native Clients
docs.text: Use PKCE for public clients to prevent code interception attacks.

docs.title: 23.3 Protected Resource Metadata
docs.text: Use metadata discovery to configure endpoints and scopes automatically.

docs.title: 23.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Enterprise MCP host discovers auth metadata and completes secure sign-in.

docs.title: 24 - Security Hardening, Threat Modeling, and Mitigation
24.1 Threat Model
docs.text: Assess spoofing, prompt injection, data exfiltration, and tool abuse risks.

docs.title: 24.2 Hardening Controls
docs.text: Implement authz boundaries, schema guards, sandboxing, and audit logging.

docs.title: 24.3 Secure Defaults
docs.text: Default to least privilege, explicit consent, and deny-by-default tool access.

docs.title: 24.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Red-team simulation exposes unsafe tool chaining; policy gate blocks exploit path.

docs.title: 25 - MCP Inspector, Conformance Testing, and Debugging
25.1 Inspector Workflow
docs.text: Use MCP Inspector to inspect capabilities, messages, and primitive behavior.

docs.title: 25.2 Conformance Suites
docs.text: Automate protocol and schema compliance tests in CI pipelines.

docs.title: 25.3 Debugging Patterns
docs.text: Trace failed method calls with request IDs and lifecycle checkpoints.

docs.title: 25.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: A regression in tool schema is caught before release by inspector-based tests.

docs.title: 26 - MCP Registry, Discovery, and Package Distribution
26.1 Registry Fundamentals
docs.text: Publish discoverable MCP servers with clear metadata and version labels.

docs.title: 26.2 Discovery Strategy
docs.text: Use search filters, trust policies, and compatibility metadata.

docs.title: 26.3 Distribution Operations
docs.text: Version packages consistently and verify integrity before install.

docs.title: 26.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Team deploys curated internal registry for approved finance-domain servers.

docs.title: 27 - Tasks, Extensions, and MCP Apps: Advanced Patterns
27.1 Task-Oriented Design
docs.text: Use task abstractions for long-running multi-step workflows.

docs.title: 27.2 Extension Architecture
docs.text: Isolate extensions with explicit contracts and lifecycle hooks.

docs.title: 27.3 App Integration Patterns
docs.text: Compose MCP primitives into complete domain-specific applications.

docs.title: 27.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Compliance app orchestrates document review and approval tasks via MCP.

docs.title: 28 - Production Deployment, Scaling, and Observability for MCP
28.1 Deployment Topologies
docs.text: Choose local, edge, or centralized MCP server deployment by latency and control needs.

docs.title: 28.2 Scaling Strategy
docs.text: Scale stateless components horizontally and isolate stateful services.

docs.title: 28.3 Observability Stack
docs.text: Collect logs, traces, metrics, and protocol-level audit events.

docs.title: 28.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Traffic spikes are absorbed through autoscaling and rate-limited tool execution.

docs.title: 29 - Incident Response, Governance, and Operational Excellence
29.1 Incident Playbooks
docs.text: Define detection, triage, containment, and recovery for MCP failures.

docs.title: 29.2 Governance Model
docs.text: Set ownership, policy controls, review cycles, and approval workflows.

docs.title: 29.3 Operational KPIs
docs.text: Track uptime, mean time to detect, mean time to recover, and policy violations.

docs.title: 29.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Unauthorized tool-call burst is detected and blocked with automatic incident escalation.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: MCP Platform System Design
30.1 Capstone Goal
docs.text: Design enterprise MCP platform architecture covering protocol, security, and operations.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text: Provide architecture diagram, capability matrix, auth model, and SLO plan.

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text: Assess correctness, resilience, security posture, and production readiness.

docs.title: 30.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Capstone team presents a multi-tenant MCP platform for regulated support automation.

docs.title: Model Context Protocol for AI
docs.text: This folder contains a complete Foundations + Advanced documentation set.

docs.title: Open First
docs.text: • INDEX.md

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
1. Foundations Track: chapters 01-15
  2. Advanced Track: chapters 16-30

docs.title: 1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build complete production-ready MLOps capabilities for reliable ML systems.

docs.title: 1.2 Lifecycle
docs.text: Design -> data -> train -> validate -> deploy -> monitor -> improve.

docs.title: 1.3 Core Pillars
docs.text: Reproducibility, reliability, governance, and continuous improvement.

docs.title: 1.4 Operating Model
docs.text: Cross-functional collaboration between data science, platform, and SRE teams.

docs.title: 1.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Fraud model updates run through automated train-validate-deploy pipeline weekly.

docs.title: 2.1 Environment Strategy
docs.text: Separate dev, test, staging, and production with strict boundary controls.

docs.title: 2.2 Core Components
docs.text: Data platform, orchestration, registry, serving stack, and observability systems.

docs.title: 2.3 Infrastructure Baseline
docs.text: Use IaC, secrets management, and standardized runtime images.

docs.title: 2.4 Reproducibility Foundation
docs.text: Pin dependencies and runtime configuration across environments.

docs.title: 2.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Training jobs run in staging mirror before production promotion.

docs.title: 3.1 Data Versioning
docs.text: Track exact training and validation data snapshots.

docs.title: 3.2 Lineage Model
docs.text: Link datasets to features, models, experiments, and deployments.

docs.title: 3.3 Quality Controls
docs.text: Validate schema, null rates, drift signals, and label integrity.

docs.title: 3.4 Governance
docs.text: Assign data ownership and change-approval workflows.

docs.title: 3.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Regression issue is traced to one mislabeled data snapshot version.

docs.title: 4.1 Pipeline Scope
docs.text: Automate ingestion, preprocessing, feature generation, and model training.

docs.title: 4.2 Orchestration
docs.text: Use DAG workflows with retries, checkpoints, and dependency controls.

docs.title: 4.3 Failure Handling
docs.text: Implement idempotent tasks and dead-letter handling.

docs.title: 4.4 Scheduling Strategy
docs.text: Combine periodic retraining and event-driven retraining triggers.

docs.title: 4.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Demand forecast model retrains daily with automated pipeline validation.

docs.title: 05 - Feature Engineering and Feature Store Patterns
5.1 Feature Lifecycle
docs.text: Design, validate, register, and serve reusable features.

docs.title: 5.2 Offline/Online Sync
docs.text: Ensure training-serving consistency through feature store design.

docs.title: 5.3 Feature Governance
docs.text: Track ownership, definitions, and deprecation policies.

docs.title: 5.4 Quality Signals
docs.text: Monitor feature freshness, skew, and stability.

docs.title: 5.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Realtime fraud features stay consistent with offline training snapshots.

docs.title: 6.1 Tracking Scope
docs.text: Log hyperparameters, datasets, code versions, metrics, and artifacts.

docs.title: 6.2 Reproducibility
docs.text: Recreate training outcomes using exact run metadata.

docs.title: 6.3 Comparison Framework
docs.text: Compare runs by quality, fairness, latency, and resource use.

docs.title: 6.4 Promotion Rules
docs.text: Advance only runs that satisfy technical and policy thresholds.

docs.title: 6.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Best run is promoted after passing reproducibility and fairness checks.

docs.title: 7.1 Registry Role
docs.text: Centralize model artifacts and lifecycle state transitions.

docs.title: 7.2 Version Governance
docs.text: Use immutable versions with staging and production promotion states.

docs.title: 7.3 Approval Workflow
docs.text: Require technical, risk, and business approvals for release.

docs.title: 7.4 Rollback Readiness
docs.text: Maintain known-good production versions for rapid rollback.

docs.title: 7.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Model v12 rollout is reverted to v11 after production drift alert.

docs.title: 08 - Model Validation, Benchmarking, and Approval Gates
8.1 Validation Dimensions
docs.text: Accuracy, calibration, fairness, robustness, and stability.

docs.title: 8.2 Benchmark Sets
docs.text: Use representative and adversarial datasets for comprehensive validation.

docs.title: 8.3 Approval Gates
docs.text: Define mandatory pass criteria before deployment.

docs.title: 8.4 Governance
docs.text: Document exceptions and compensating controls when thresholds are missed.

docs.title: 8.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Model fails release gate due to fairness disparity across regions.

docs.title: 09 - Deployment Patterns: Batch, Online, and Streaming
9.1 Batch Inference
docs.text: Best for periodic scoring with large volume and lower latency demands.

docs.title: 9.2 Online Serving
docs.text: Supports low-latency request-response inference.

docs.title: 9.3 Streaming Inference
docs.text: Handles continuous event flows with near-real-time predictions.

docs.title: 9.4 Pattern Selection
docs.text: Choose based on SLA, cost, and freshness requirements.

docs.title: 9.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Fraud detection combines online scoring with nightly batch recalibration.

docs.title: 10.1 CI Layer
docs.text: Automate code quality, unit tests, and data contract checks.

docs.title: 10.2 CD Layer
docs.text: Automate deployment promotion with canary and rollback mechanisms.

docs.title: 10.3 CT Layer
docs.text: Continuously retrain models when triggers or schedules are met.

docs.title: 10.4 Gate Integration
docs.text: Embed evaluation and policy checks in every pipeline stage.

docs.title: 10.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Pipeline retrains and redeploys recommendation model after feature drift trigger.

docs.title: 11 - Monitoring: Data Drift, Model Drift, and Performance
11.1 Drift Types
docs.text: Monitor covariate drift, concept drift, and prediction drift.

docs.title: 11.2 Performance Signals
docs.text: Track latency, accuracy decay, and business KPI movement.

docs.title: 11.3 Detection Methods
docs.text: Use statistical tests and threshold-based anomaly detection.

docs.title: 11.4 Action Policies
docs.text: Define retraining, rollback, or alert escalation responses.

docs.title: 11.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Model retraining is triggered after sustained drift in transaction patterns.

docs.title: 12.1 Alert Design
docs.text: Create actionable alerts with severity, impact, and owner metadata.

docs.title: 12.2 Incident Workflow
docs.text: Triage, contain, mitigate, recover, and postmortem.

docs.title: 12.3 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Use fallback models, circuit breakers, and graceful degradation.

docs.title: 12.4 Learning Loop
docs.text: Convert incidents into test and runbook improvements.

docs.title: 12.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Inference service falls back to baseline model during feature store outage.

docs.title: 13.1 Security Baseline
docs.text: Protect pipelines, model endpoints, and artifacts with least privilege.

docs.title: 13.2 Privacy Controls
docs.text: Apply masking, retention, and deletion policies for sensitive data.

docs.title: 13.3 Compliance Mapping
docs.text: Map controls to regulatory and internal governance requirements.

docs.title: 13.4 Auditability
docs.text: Maintain immutable evidence for decisions and model changes.

docs.title: 13.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Sensitive features are tokenized before training and inference processing.

docs.title: 14.1 Cost Drivers
docs.text: Compute, storage, inference traffic, and retraining frequency.

docs.title: 14.2 Optimization Levers
docs.text: Right-size infrastructure, schedule jobs efficiently, and optimize model footprint.

docs.title: 14.3 Resource Governance
docs.text: Set budgets and chargeback policies per team or product.

docs.title: 14.4 Tradeoff Strategy
docs.text: Balance performance improvements against incremental spend.

docs.title: 14.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Autoscaling policy reduces idle inference cost without SLA impact.

docs.title: 15 - Labs, Projects, Glossary, and Operational Checklists
Lab 1
docs.text: Implement reproducible experiment pipeline with versioned data and model artifacts.

docs.title: Lab 2
docs.text: Build CI/CD/CT workflow with validation and canary release gates.

docs.title: Lab 3
docs.text: Set up drift monitoring with automatic retraining trigger.

docs.title: Lab 4
docs.text: Run incident simulation and document reliability improvements.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
• model registry
  • drift
  • feature store
  • canary
  • error budget

docs.title: Pre-Release Checklist
docs.text:
• validation gates pass
  • security policies enforced
  • monitoring dashboards active
  • rollback plan tested

docs.title: 16 - Advanced Data Contracts, Quality Gates, and Observability
16.1 Contract Strategy
docs.text: Define producer-consumer data contracts with schema and semantic guarantees.

docs.title: 16.2 Quality Gates
docs.text: Enforce contract checks before training and inference pipeline execution.

docs.title: 16.3 Observability
docs.text: Track contract violations, freshness issues, and schema drift trends.

docs.title: 16.4 Incident Linkage
docs.text: Route data quality incidents into reliability workflows.

docs.title: 16.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Training job is blocked automatically after upstream schema breaking change.

docs.title: 17 - Advanced Workflow Orchestration and Dependency Management
17.1 DAG Governance
docs.text: Design modular DAGs with clear dependencies and ownership boundaries.

docs.title: 17.2 Dynamic Workflows
docs.text: Support conditional retraining and branch-specific execution paths.

docs.title: 17.3 Dependency Controls
docs.text: Pin upstream versions and validate external dependency health.

docs.title: 17.4 Recovery Patterns
docs.text: Resume from checkpoints and isolate failed branches.

docs.title: 17.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Retraining workflow skips unaffected feature branches during partial source outage.

docs.title: 18 - Distributed Training, HPO, and Experiment Scaling
18.1 Distributed Strategy
docs.text: Scale training across nodes with deterministic checkpointing.

docs.title: 18.2 HPO Design
docs.text: Run Bayesian or bandit-based optimization with budget constraints.

docs.title: 18.3 Experiment Governance
docs.text: Track massive parallel runs with lineage and reproducibility controls.

docs.title: 18.4 Resource Efficiency
docs.text: Schedule GPU/CPU usage to minimize idle and contention.

docs.title: 18.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: HPO pipeline finds better fraud model while reducing GPU spend by pruning weak trials.

docs.title: 19 - Advanced Feature Platform: RealTime and Offline Consistency
19.1 Consistency Objective
docs.text: Ensure training and serving use semantically identical feature definitions.

docs.title: 19.2 Synchronization Model
docs.text: Coordinate backfills, streaming updates, and point-in-time correctness.

docs.title: 19.3 Validation
docs.text: Detect feature skew and stale-value anomalies.

docs.title: 19.4 Governance
docs.text: Version feature transformations and deprecate safely.

docs.title: 19.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Online recommendation drift traced to offline-online feature mismatch and corrected.

docs.title: 20 - Advanced Validation, Fairness, Robustness, and Explainability
20.1 Validation Depth
docs.text: Assess subgroup fairness, robustness under shift, and explanation quality.

docs.title: 20.2 Robustness Testing
docs.text: Use stress tests and adversarial slices beyond standard benchmarks.

docs.title: 20.3 Explainability Controls
docs.text: Provide interpretable outputs for high-impact decisions.

docs.title: 20.4 Governance Gates
docs.text: Require fairness and robustness thresholds before release.

docs.title: 20.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Credit model release is delayed until subgroup disparity is remediated.

docs.title: 21 - Progressive Delivery: Shadow, Canary, and Champion-Challenger
21.1 Shadow Deployment
docs.text: Run model in parallel without affecting live decisions.

docs.title: 21.2 Canary Deployment
docs.text: Gradually increase live traffic while monitoring key risk metrics.

docs.title: 21.3 Champion-Challenger
docs.text: Continuously compare incumbent and candidate models.

docs.title: 21.4 Rollback Logic
docs.text: Automate rollback on quality, latency, or safety regression.

docs.title: 21.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Challenger model overtakes champion after consistent uplift in conversion and fairness.

docs.title: 22 - Inference Optimization, Scaling, and Latency Engineering
22.1 Optimization Levers
docs.text: Apply batching, quantization, distillation, and runtime acceleration.

docs.title: 22.2 Scaling Strategy
docs.text: Scale endpoints horizontally and isolate high-latency dependencies.

docs.title: 22.3 Latency Budgeting
docs.text: Allocate per-component latency targets and enforce p95 SLOs.

docs.title: 22.4 Capacity Controls
docs.text: Autoscale by demand while preserving cost guardrails.

docs.title: 22.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Quantized model variant meets mobile latency SLO without accuracy breach.

docs.title: 23 - Deep Observability, Traces, Metrics, and System Forensics
23.1 Telemetry Model
docs.text: Instrument training, serving, and feature pipelines end-to-end.

docs.title: 23.2 Forensics Workflow
docs.text: Correlate incidents with model versions, data slices, and infrastructure events.

docs.title: 23.3 Diagnostic Depth
docs.text: Capture fine-grained traces for root-cause analysis.

docs.title: 23.4 Continuous Monitoring
docs.text: Detect anomalies early with predictive alerts.

docs.title: 23.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Forensic trace links prediction drift to delayed feature stream ingestion.

docs.title: 24 - Automated Recovery, Rollback, and Self-Healing Patterns
24.1 Recovery Automation
docs.text: Trigger predefined mitigation actions when thresholds are breached.

docs.title: 24.2 Rollback Strategies
docs.text: Support model, feature, and config rollback independently.

docs.title: 24.3 Self-Healing Logic
docs.text: Auto-switch to safe fallback models and degraded feature sets.

docs.title: 24.4 Validation
docs.text: Confirm post-recovery stability before resuming normal operations.

docs.title: 24.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: System auto-reverts to prior model after spike in false positives.

docs.title: 25 - Governance, Model Risk Management, and Auditability
25.1 Risk Framework
docs.text: Classify models by impact and define control rigor by tier.

docs.title: 25.2 Governance Workflows
docs.text: Require documented approvals and periodic model reviews.

docs.title: 25.3 Audit Artifacts
docs.text: Store immutable evidence for training, validation, and deployment decisions.

docs.title: 25.4 Control Testing
docs.text: Regularly test governance controls for effectiveness.

docs.title: 25.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Annual audit reconstructs full lineage of production credit scoring model.

docs.title: 26 - Multi-Tenant ML Platform Architecture and Isolation
26.1 Isolation Design
docs.text: Separate tenants across data, features, models, and runtime credentials.

docs.title: 26.2 Shared Control Plane
docs.text: Centralize governance while preserving tenant autonomy.

docs.title: 26.3 Cost Allocation
docs.text: Track usage and chargeback per tenant or business unit.

docs.title: 26.4 Compliance
docs.text: Support residency and tenant-specific policy overrides.

docs.title: 26.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Platform enforces strict namespace isolation for regulated banking tenants.

docs.title: 27 - MLOps for Foundation and Multimodal Models
27.1 Scope Expansion
docs.text: Extend MLOps patterns to text, image, audio, and video model pipelines.

docs.title: 27.2 Artifact Management
docs.text: Version prompts/adapters alongside model and data artifacts.

docs.title: 27.3 Evaluation Complexity
docs.text: Measure modality-specific quality and cross-modal consistency.

docs.title: 27.4 Runtime Considerations
docs.text: Handle larger models with optimized serving and observability.

docs.title: 27.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Document-understanding pipeline combines OCR, vision model, and classifier stages.

docs.title: 28 - Hybrid Cloud, Edge, and Federated MLOps Patterns
28.1 Hybrid Strategy
docs.text: Distribute training and inference across cloud, on-prem, and edge environments.

docs.title: 28.2 Federated Patterns
docs.text: Coordinate decentralized learning with privacy-preserving aggregation.

docs.title: 28.3 Deployment Governance
docs.text: Unify release controls across heterogeneous infrastructure.

docs.title: 28.4 Reliability Controls
docs.text: Design offline-tolerant and sync-safe edge operations.

docs.title: 28.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Retail edge models run locally and sync updates centrally overnight.

docs.title: 29.1 Migration Strategy
docs.text: Plan phased migrations across tooling, models, and platform components.

docs.title: 29.2 Compatibility Matrix
docs.text: Track backward compatibility for APIs, features, and model contracts.

docs.title: 29.3 Lifecycle Governance
docs.text: Define deprecation windows and retirement process.

docs.title: 29.4 Risk Mitigation
docs.text: Use shadow runs and rollback checkpoints during migration.

docs.title: 29.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Serving platform upgrade runs in parallel mode before production cutover.

docs.title: 30 - Advanced Capstone: Design a Production MLOps Platform
30.1 Capstone Goal
docs.text: Design a resilient, governed, and scalable enterprise MLOps platform.

docs.title: 30.2 Required Deliverables
docs.text: Reference architecture, control plane design, reliability model, and KPI/SLO framework.

docs.title: 30.3 Evaluation Rubric
docs.text: Assess quality assurance, governance depth, scalability, and operational readiness.

docs.title: 30.4 Failure Simulation
docs.text: Include rollback, disaster recovery, and postmortem improvement workflow.

docs.title: 30.5 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Capstone platform supports regulated real-time risk scoring with full audit traceability.

docs.title: MLOps for AI
docs.text: This folder contains a complete Foundations + Advanced documentation set.

docs.title: Open First
docs.text: • INDEX.md

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
1. Foundations Track: chapters 01-15
  2. Advanced Track: chapters 16-30

docs.title: 1.1 Goal
docs.text: Build practical, production-ready machine learning skills for AI systems.

docs.title: 1.2 ML Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. problem framing
  2. data preparation
  3. model training
  4. evaluation
  5. deployment
  6. monitoring and iteration

docs.title: 25.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Distilled support model halves latency with minimal quality loss.

docs.title: 26.1 Multimodal NLP
docs.text: Combine text with speech, vision, and structured context.

docs.title: 26.2 Speech Pipelines
docs.text: ASR -> language understanding -> response generation.

docs.title: 26.3 Challenges
docs.text:
• latency
  • alignment across modalities
  • error propagation

docs.title: 26.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Voice support assistant transcribes calls and creates action summaries.

docs.title: 27 - Fairness, Privacy, and Governance in NLP
27.1 Fairness Review
docs.text: Evaluate subgroup performance and harmful output patterns.

docs.title: 27.2 Privacy Controls
docs.text:
• PII detection/masking
  • secure logging policies
  • retention and deletion rules

docs.title: 27.3 Governance
docs.text: Use model cards, prompt cards, and release approval workflows.

docs.title: 27.4 Real-Time Example
docs.text: PII redaction layer blocks sensitive data in generated responses.

docs.title: 28 - Monitoring, Drift, and Incident Response for NLP
28.1 Monitoring Signals
docs.text:
• input drift
  • output quality drift
  • retrieval grounding failures
  • policy violation rates

docs.title: 28.2 Incident Runbook
docs.text:
1. detect issue
  2. reduce impact (fallback/rollback)
  3. diagnose root cause
  4. patch and validate

docs.title: 28.3 Real-Time Example
docs.text: Rollback prompt version after safety violation spike.

docs.title: 29.1 System Architecture
docs.text:
• preprocessing service
  • model service
  • retrieval/index service
  • monitoring layer

docs.title: 29.2 Deployment Patterns
docs.text: canary, shadow, blue/green with benchmark-based release gates.

docs.title: 29.3 Operations
docs.text: track latency, cost, quality, and safety metrics continuously.

docs.title: 29.4 Real-Time Example
